Essential for integrated ticket and ancillary revenue reporting.']
  station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Ancillary orders are sold and fulfilled at specific stations. Critical for station-level revenue reporting, inventory management, cost allocation, and operational performance tracking. Aviation domain']
  base_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total base price of all ancillary services in the order before taxes, fees, and discounts. Represents the gross ancillary revenue component.']
  booking_class varchar [note: 'Single-letter fare class code of the base ticket to which this ancillary order is linked. Used for bundled fare product eligibility and ancillary pricing rules.']
  cabin_class varchar [note: 'Cabin class of the base ticket to which this ancillary order is linked. Influences ancillary product eligibility and pricing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ancillary order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the ancillary order was priced and paid (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled departure date of the flight to which the ancillary service applies. Null if the ancillary is not flight-specific.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code representing the destination of the journey for which the ancillary service was purchased. Null if the ancillary is not flight-specific.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the ancillary order, including promotional discounts, loyalty member discounts, and bundled fare reductions.']
  emd_number varchar [note: 'Thirteen-digit EMD number issued for this ancillary order. Links the order to the official IATA-compliant revenue document for settlement and reporting.']
  emd_type varchar [note: 'Type of EMD issued: EMD-S (standalone, not linked to a flight coupon) or EMD-A (associated with a specific flight coupon).']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total fees charged on the ancillary order, including booking fees, service charges, and payment processing fees.']
  fulfillment_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ancillary service was delivered or consumed by the passenger. Null if not yet fulfilled.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the ancillary service has been delivered to the passenger (e.g., baggage checked, seat assigned, meal served).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ancillary order record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ancillary order was placed by the passenger or agent. Represents the principal business event time for revenue recognition.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique order number assigned to this ancillary purchase. Used for customer service, refunds, and revenue reconciliation.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ancillary order. Tracks progression from initial booking through fulfillment or cancellation.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code representing the origin of the journey for which the ancillary service was purchased. Null if the ancillary is not flight-specific.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used to settle the ancillary order. Distinguishes between card payments, electronic transfers, loyalty miles, and other tender types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|bank_transfer|paypal|miles_redemption|voucher|cash|corporate_account — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or transaction identifier from the payment gateway or processor. Used for payment reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment transaction for this ancillary order. Tracks payment lifecycle from authorization through settlement or refund. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|authorized|captured|settled|failed|refunded|partially_refunded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  point_of_sale_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the ancillary order was sold. Used for revenue allocation and tax jurisdiction determination.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Promotional or discount code applied to the ancillary order. Used for marketing campaign tracking and discount validation.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount refunded to the passenger for this ancillary order. Null if no refund has been processed.']
  refund_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the refund was processed for this ancillary order. Null if no refund has been issued.']
  refund_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ancillary order is eligible for refund based on fare rules, timing, and fulfillment status.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total taxes applied to the ancillary order, including sales tax, value-added tax (VAT), goods and services tax (GST), and other jurisdiction-specific levies.']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net total amount payable for the ancillary order after applying taxes, fees, and discounts. Represents the final revenue recognized for this transaction.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a passenger\'s purchase of one or more ancillary services, linked to a PNR and e-ticket. Captures order date, sales channel (GDS, NDC, direct, airport), total amount, currency, payment reference, order status, and the originating booking context. Acts as the commercial anchor for ancillary revenue recognition and EMD issuance.'
}

Table ancillary.order_item {
  order_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for order_item']
  ancillary_emd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.ancillary_emd. Business justification: Order items can be documented via EMD issuance. The order_item table has emd_number (STRING) but no FK. Adding this FK links order fulfillment to EMD documentation for settlement and accounting.']
  ancillary_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent ancillary order header that contains this line item. Links the item to its order context.']
  booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.booking_passenger. Business justification: Associates order items with booking passengers for item-level passenger attribution, service fulfillment, and purchase history. Required for passenger-specific ancillary sales analysis.']
  checkin_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.checkin_session. Business justification: Excess baggage fees, seat selection, and special service ancillaries are purchased during check-in. Linking order_item to checkin_session enables check-in channel ancillary revenue attribution, baggag']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Line-item level company code attribution enables accurate revenue allocation in multi-entity airline groups, especially for codeshare and interline ancillary sales where different items may belong to ']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Ancillary revenue line items must be attributed to cost centres for airline management accounting (CASK/RASK reporting by business unit). Aviation controllers require cost centre on order items to all']
  fare_class_bucket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.fare_class_bucket. Business justification: Ancillary attach rate analysis by fare class bucket is a core airline RM report — identifying which RBDs generate highest ancillary revenue per passenger. order_item has booking_class as a plain denor']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: Ancillary order items (seat selection, baggage, meals) are sold against specific flight inventory. Revenue management and inventory control require tracking which flight\'s inventory each ancillary sal']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.flight_leg. Business justification: Ancillary service fulfillment (seat assignment, meal delivery, baggage handling) occurs on the actual operating flight. Linking order_item to flight_leg enables ground ops and cabin crew to reconcile ']
  fulfillment_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Order items have station-specific fulfillment points (baggage charges at check-in, lounge access at specific airports). Essential for station performance tracking, revenue attribution by location, and']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Each ancillary order line item (base_amount, tax_amount, commission_amount) posts to a specific GL account for granular revenue recognition. Aviation revenue accounting requires line-item GL attributi']
  itinerary_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.itinerary_segment. Business justification: Links ancillary order items to specific flight segments for segment-level revenue attribution, service delivery, and operational reporting. Required for per-segment ancillary sales analysis.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific passenger who is the recipient or beneficiary of this ancillary service.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.pnr. Business justification: Enables item-level traceability to booking for ancillary revenue attribution, refund processing, and passenger service history. Required for detailed ancillary sales reporting by booking.']
  product_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific ancillary product or service purchased in this line item from the ancillary product catalog.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Order items must link to route for ancillary revenue allocation, operational fulfillment planning, and route profitability analysis. Real business process: ancillary revenue reporting by route, servic']
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight leg or segment to which this ancillary service applies. Nullable for non-flight-specific services.']
  terminal_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.terminal_facility. Business justification: Lounge access, fast-track security, and terminal retail ancillary items are fulfilled at specific terminal facilities. This link enables terminal-level ancillary revenue reporting and facility utiliza']
  base_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total base amount for this line item (unit price multiplied by quantity), excluding taxes and fees.']
  booking_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ancillary service was booked or added to the order, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  commission_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Commission amount payable to the sales agent or distribution channel for this ancillary line item.']
  commission_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission rate as a percentage of the base amount payable to the sales agent or distribution channel.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation or reference number issued upon successful booking of the ancillary service.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this order item record was first created in the system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this line item (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the destination of the flight segment associated with this ancillary item.']
  emd_type varchar [note: 'Type of EMD issued: EMD-S (standalone, not linked to a ticket) or EMD-A (associated with an e-ticket).']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional fees or surcharges applied to this ancillary line item (e.g., processing fees, service charges).']
  flight_date date [note: 'Scheduled departure date of the flight to which this ancillary service applies, in yyyy-MM-dd format.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number (carrier code + numeric flight identifier) to which this ancillary service is linked, if applicable.']
  fulfilment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier used by operational systems to track delivery or fulfilment of the ancillary service (e.g., baggage tag number, seat assignment, lounge access code).']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the EMD or service document was issued for this ancillary item, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number of this item within the parent ancillary order. Used for display and processing order.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the origin of the flight segment associated with this ancillary item.']
  passenger_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the passenger associated with this ancillary service item, as recorded in the PNR.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Business code identifying the ancillary product type. May align with RFIC (Reason for Issuance Code) and RFISC (Reason for Issuance Sub-Code) standards.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the ancillary service purchased, such as \'Extra Baggage 23kg\', \'Premium Seat 12A\', \'Priority Boarding\', or \'Wi-Fi Access\'.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity or count of the ancillary service purchased (e.g., 2 bags, 1 seat, 3 meal vouchers).']
  refund_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ancillary service is eligible for refund according to the fare rules and service terms. True if refundable, False if non-refundable.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the ancillary service was sold: web (airline website), mobile_app (airline mobile application), airport_counter (airport check-in or sales desk), call_center (reservation center), gds (Global Distribution System), travel_agent (third-party agent), or kiosk (self-service kiosk). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|airport_counter|call_center|gds|travel_agent|kiosk — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the ancillary service: confirmed (service guaranteed), waitlisted (pending availability), cancelled (service cancelled), refunded (payment returned), pending (awaiting confirmation), or delivered (service fulfilled).']
  ssr_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter IATA SSR code representing the ancillary service type (e.g., BAGD for baggage, SEAT for seat selection, MEAL for meal preference).']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to this ancillary line item, including VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes.']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount for this line item including base amount, taxes, and fees. Represents the full revenue for this ancillary service.']
  unit_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Price per unit of the ancillary service before taxes and fees, in the transaction currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this order item record was last modified in the system, in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail of each ancillary service within an ancillary order. Captures the specific ancillary product purchased, quantity, unit price, total amount, applicable flight segment, passenger association, SSR code, service status (confirmed, waitlisted, cancelled), and fulfilment reference. Enables per-service revenue tracking and operational delivery coordination.'
}

Table ancillary.ancillary_emd {
  ancillary_emd_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Electronic Miscellaneous Document record. Primary key for the ancillary EMD product.']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: EMDs issued on credit terms create receivables requiring tracking and collection. Essential for BSP billing, agency debt management, and reconciliation of EMD issuance with cash collection.']
  ancillary_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.ancillary_order. Business justification: ancillary_emd is an Electronic Miscellaneous Document issued for ancillary services sold. ancillary_order is the transactional purchase record that triggers EMD issuance. Linking ancillary_emd to its ']
  booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.booking_passenger. Business justification: Links EMDs to booking passengers for passenger-specific EMD issuance, service entitlement, and refund processing. Essential for EMD passenger association and service delivery.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: EMDs are financial instruments requiring legal entity attribution for statutory reporting, tax compliance, and BSP/ARC settlement. Each EMD must be issued by a specific company code for regulatory and']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: EMD financial values (face_value_amount, commission_amount) must be attributed to cost centres for airline management accounting. Aviation controllers allocate EMD revenue to issuing station or produc']
  e_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.e_ticket. Business justification: IATA EMD standard requires EMDs to reference the conjunction e-ticket for BSP settlement and revenue accounting. An EMD is always issued in association with a specific ticket. Aviation revenue account']
  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.flight_leg. Business justification: IATA EMD coupon status (open/used/void) must be reconciled against the operated flight leg for revenue accounting and regulatory compliance. Revenue accounting teams require EMD-to-flight-leg traceabi']
  flight_number_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.flight_number. Business justification: EMDs (Electronic Miscellaneous Documents) are issued against specific flight numbers per IATA Resolution 636 and BSP/ARC settlement rules. The existing flight_number column is denormalized text. A rev']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: EMDs are IATA financial instruments; each EMD issuance, exchange, or refund posts to GL for revenue recognition and BSP settlement reconciliation. Aviation finance requires direct GL linkage on EMDs f']
  itinerary_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.itinerary_segment. Business justification: IATA EMD coupons are segment-specific (e.g., excess baggage on a particular flight leg). Interline EMD settlement and proration require the EMD to reference the specific itinerary segment. ancillary_e']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.pnr. Business justification: Links EMDs to booking records for IATA BSP/ARC settlement, revenue accounting, and EMD-to-ticket association. Mandatory for EMD lifecycle management and financial reconciliation.']
  ticket_coupon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.ticket_coupon. Business justification: IATA EMD standard requires EMDs to be associated with specific ticket coupons (EMD-A \'associated\' type). BSP settlement and revenue accounting depend on this coupon-level EMD linkage. An IATA revenue ']
  ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.ticket. Business justification: EMDs (Electronic Miscellaneous Documents) are issued in conjunction with tickets for ancillary services. This FK links the ancillary EMD to the base ticket for complete passenger revenue tracking and ']
  ancillary_service_code varchar [note: 'IATA RFIC (Reason for Issuance Code) or RFISC (Reason for Issuance Sub-Code) identifying the specific ancillary service. Examples: 0CC=baggage, 0B5=seat selection, 0G1=lounge access.']
  commission_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Commission amount payable to the issuing agent or agency for selling the ancillary service. Used for agent settlement and revenue accounting.']
  commission_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission rate as a percentage of the face value. Used to calculate agent compensation for ancillary sales.']
  coupon_number varchar [note: 'Sequential coupon number within the EMD. An EMD may have multiple coupons for multi-segment or multi-service purchases. Typically 1 for single-service EMDs.']
  coupon_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the individual EMD coupon. Tracks the lifecycle state of each service component within the EMD. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|used|refunded|exchanged|void|airport_control|checked_in|flown|suspended|irregular — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 3-letter currency code in which the EMD was issued. Identifies the monetary unit for all amounts on the document.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'IATA 3-letter airport code of the destination for the service. Identifies where the ancillary service ends or is delivered.']
  emd_number varchar [note: '13-digit IATA-standard EMD number uniquely identifying the document globally. Format follows IATA Resolution 722 structure with airline code prefix and sequential number.']
  emd_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the EMD coupon. Open indicates unused, used indicates service consumed, refunded indicates money returned, exchanged indicates replaced with new EMD, void indicates cancelled before use, expired indicates validity period lapsed.']
  emd_type varchar [note: 'Type of EMD issued. EMD-S (standalone) is issued independently for ancillary services. EMD-A (associated) is linked to a base e-ticket for bundled services.']
  endorsement_restrictions varchar [note: 'Free-text field containing endorsement and restriction information. Specifies conditions, limitations, or special handling instructions for the EMD.']
  exchange_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the EMD is exchangeable. True if the ancillary service can be exchanged for another service or flight, false if non-exchangeable.']
  face_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total face value amount of the EMD in the currency of issuance. Represents the gross charge for the ancillary service before any adjustments or taxes.']
  form_of_payment varchar [note: 'Detailed form of payment string including masked card number or payment reference. Format follows IATA FOP standards. Contains partial payment card data.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the EMD was issued. Marks the creation of the document and start of validity period.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the EMD was issued in the issuing system. Used for audit trail and transaction sequencing.']
  issuing_airline_code varchar [note: 'IATA 2-letter or 3-letter airline code of the carrier that issued the EMD. Identifies the airline responsible for the document and settlement.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'IATA 3-letter airport code of the origin for the service. Identifies where the ancillary service begins or is consumed.']
  original_issue_date date [note: 'Original issue date for exchanged or reissued EMDs. Tracks the first issuance date when an EMD has been modified or replaced.']
  passenger_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the passenger for whom the ancillary service EMD was issued. Format typically follows surname/given name convention per IATA standards.']
  passenger_type_code varchar [note: 'IATA passenger type code. ADT=adult, CHD=child, INF=infant, SRC=senior citizen, MIL=military, STU=student. Determines eligibility for certain ancillary services and pricing.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method of payment used to purchase the EMD. Identifies the financial instrument used for settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|credit_card|debit_card|miles|voucher|corporate_account|bank_transfer — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refund_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the EMD is refundable. True if the ancillary service can be refunded per fare rules, false if non-refundable.']
  service_date date [note: 'Date on which the ancillary service is scheduled to be consumed or delivered. For flight-related services, typically matches the flight departure date.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the ancillary service purchased. Examples: Extra Baggage 23kg, Premium Seat 12A, Priority Boarding, Lounge Access.']
  service_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the ancillary service purchased. For example, number of baggage pieces, number of meal vouchers, or 1 for single-use services like lounge access.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount included in the EMD. Sum of all applicable taxes such as VAT, GST, or service taxes on the ancillary service.']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount payable for the EMD including face value, taxes, and any fees. This is the final charge to the passenger.']
  tour_code varchar [note: 'Tour or promotional code associated with the EMD. Used for tracking special offers, packages, or corporate agreements.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Last date on which the EMD is valid for use. After this date, the EMD expires and cannot be consumed or refunded.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'First date on which the EMD is valid for use. Defines the beginning of the service consumption window.']

  Note: 'Electronic Miscellaneous Document (EMD) master record issued for ancillary services sold separately from the base e-ticket. Captures EMD number (13-digit IATA format), EMD type (EMD-S standalone or EMD-A associated), issuing airline, issuing office/GDS, associated ticket number, passenger name, total face value, currency, issuance date, validity period, and current coupon status. SSOT for EMD lifecycle management.'
}

Table ancillary.seat_product {
  seat_product_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the commercial seat product definition. Primary key for the seat product catalog.']
  aircraft_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Seat products (sellable seat types) are aircraft-type-specific for merchandising and pricing. Airlines price "A350 Premium Economy" differently from "777 Premium Economy" due to seat specifications. R']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: Seat products are cabin-specific (business extra legroom vs economy preferred seats). Seat merchandising and inventory control require formal cabin class linkage for product applicability and pricing.']
  cabin_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.cabin_configuration. Business justification: Seat product merchandising (extra legroom, preferred seats) must reference the specific cabin configuration to identify qualifying physical seats. A single aircraft type can have multiple configuratio']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Seat products (extra legroom, preferred, exit row) are sold ancillaries requiring GL account mapping for revenue recognition. Parallel to product_catalog GL mapping; seat products need dedicated reven']
  minimum_tier_status_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier_status. Business justification: Seat product tier eligibility: seat products (exit rows, premium economy seats) are complimentary or discounted for members above a minimum tier. This FK replaces the denormalized ffp_tier_eligibility']
  product_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.product_catalog. Business justification: seat_product defines commercial seat products available for ancillary purchase. product_catalog is the master catalog of all ancillary products and services. A seat product IS an ancillary product and']
  advance_selection_window_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before departure that advance seat selection opens for this product. Null if no time restriction applies.']
  base_price_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard base price for this seat product in USD. Actual pricing may vary by route, fare class, and dynamic pricing rules. Used as reference price for revenue attribution.']
  bundle_eligibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of fare bundle codes that include this seat product as a complimentary benefit (e.g., \'BASIC,STANDARD,PREMIUM,BUSINESS\'). Empty if not included in any bundle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this seat product record was first created in the ancillary catalog system. Used for audit trail and product lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the base price (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Supports multi-currency pricing strategies.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this seat product is discontinued or removed from sale. Null for products with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this seat product becomes available for sale and merchandising. Supports seasonal product launches and fleet configuration changes.']
  emd_type varchar [note: 'Type of EMD issued for this seat product purchase. EMD-S (standalone) for ancillary-only purchases; EMD-A (associated) when linked to an e-ticket.']
  fare_class_eligibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of fare class codes eligible to purchase or select this seat product (e.g., \'Y,B,M,H,Q,V,W\'). Empty indicates available to all fare classes.']
  in_seat_entertainment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat product includes a personal in-flight entertainment (IFE) screen. Key amenity for merchandising and customer experience differentiation.']
  is_advance_selection_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether passengers can select this seat product in advance during booking or check-in. False for seats assigned only at gate or by operational staff.']
  is_bassinet_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat product supports bassinet attachment for infant passengers. Typically applies to bulkhead seats in long-haul configurations.']
  is_bulkhead boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat product is located at a bulkhead position (wall or divider in front). Affects legroom, storage, and bassinet availability.']
  is_chargeable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat product incurs a charge for passengers. False for complimentary seat assignments included in fare bundles or elite status benefits.']
  is_exit_row boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seat product is located in an emergency exit row. Triggers regulatory eligibility checks and safety briefing requirements per FAA/EASA regulations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this seat product record was last updated. Tracks pricing changes, configuration updates, and status transitions.']
  merchandising_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking for display priority in seat selection interfaces and upsell offers. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for merchandising presentation.']
  power_outlet_type varchar [note: 'Type of power outlet available at this seat product for passenger device charging. Key amenity for merchandising premium seat products.']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Revenue tier classification for this seat product. References pricing rules and fare class eligibility in revenue management systems.']
  product_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the seat product in the ancillary catalog. Controls availability for merchandising and booking channels.']
  recline_inches decimal(4,2) [note: 'Maximum recline distance of the seat in inches. Null for non-reclining seats (e.g., exit row, bulkhead).']
  refundability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seat product purchase is refundable if the passenger cancels or changes their seat selection. Subject to fare rules and timing restrictions.']
  rfisc_code varchar [note: 'Three-character IATA RFISC code identifying the ancillary service type for EMD issuance and settlement (e.g., \'0B5\' for preferred seat, \'0B6\' for extra legroom seat).']
  seat_pitch_inches decimal(5,2) [note: 'Distance from one point on a seat to the same point on the seat in front or behind, measured in inches. Key comfort metric for merchandising and customer communication.']
  seat_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the seat product defining its physical and commercial characteristics. Drives merchandising strategy and revenue attribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|preferred|extra_legroom|exit_row|bulkhead|bassinet|window|aisle|middle — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  seat_width_inches decimal(5,2) [note: 'Width of the seat measured in inches. Key comfort specification for merchandising and customer communication.']
  service_type_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter IATA service type code for ancillary categorization and reporting (e.g., \'SA\' for seat assignment, \'SB\' for seat upgrade).']
  special_service_request_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter IATA SSR code associated with this seat product for GDS and Passenger Service System (PSS) integration (e.g., \'EXST\' for extra seat, \'BLND\' for blind passenger). Null if no SSR applies.']
  transferability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seat product can be transferred to another passenger on the same Passenger Name Record (PNR) or flight. Typically false for personalized seat assignments.']
  wifi_access_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether complimentary Wi-Fi access is included with this seat product. False if Wi-Fi requires separate purchase or is not available.']
  zone_classification varchar [note: 'Cabin zone or section identifier where this seat product is typically located (e.g., \'Forward Cabin\', \'Mid Cabin\', \'Aft Cabin\', \'Zone A\', \'Zone B\'). Used for operational boarding and merchandising segmentation.']

  Note: 'Commercial seat product definitions available for ancillary purchase or upgrade, defining seat types (standard, preferred, extra legroom, exit row, bulkhead), zone classifications, pitch/width specifications, applicable aircraft types, cabin class associations, and pricing tier references. This is the sellable seat product catalog — distinct from the physical seat map (fleet domain) and operational seat assignment (reservation domain). Drives seat merchandising offers and revenue attribution by seat tier.'
}

  flight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to flight.flight_leg. Adding this FK normalizes']
  ticket_coupon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.ticket_coupon. Business justification: Seat assignments are made against specific ticket coupons in airline departure control systems. This link is required for check-in processing, boarding pass generation, and post-flight revenue settlem']
  upgrade_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.upgrade_transaction. Business justification: seat_assignment records paid or complimentary seat assignments for passengers. When a cabin upgrade is processed via upgrade_transaction, a new seat assignment is created in the upgraded cabin. Linkin']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'The method or channel through which the seat assignment was made, indicating whether it was passenger self-service, agent-assisted, or system auto-assigned. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: self_select|agent_assigned|auto_assigned|kiosk|mobile_app|web|gate — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assignment_source_system varchar [note: 'The source system or channel that originated the seat assignment transaction (PSS=Passenger Service System, DCS=Departure Control System, GDS=Global Distribution System). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pss|dcs|kiosk|mobile_app|web|gds|api — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the seat assignment indicating whether it is active, cancelled, or in transition.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the seat assignment was created or last modified. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  block_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason why the seat is blocked if is_blocked is true. Examples include crew rest, broken seat, social distancing, etc.']
  boarding_zone varchar [note: 'The boarding zone or group assigned to this seat, used for boarding sequence management. May be numeric or alphabetic depending on airline policy.']
  cabin_class_code varchar [note: 'Single-letter IATA cabin class code indicating the service class of the assigned seat (F=First, J=Business, W=Premium Economy, Y=Economy).']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this seat assignment was cancelled or released. Null if the assignment is still active. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for a seat assignment change if the seat was reassigned after initial assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: passenger_request|operational|equipment_change|overbooking|upgrade|downgrade|irop|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  check_in_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The sequence number assigned during check-in process, used for boarding pass generation and boarding order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this seat assignment record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  deck_level varchar [note: 'The deck or level of the aircraft where the seat is located. Relevant for wide-body aircraft with multiple decks.']
  group_booking_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this seat assignment is part of a group booking, which may have special handling or pricing rules.']
  infant_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether an infant (lap child) is associated with this seat assignment, which may restrict certain seat types such as exit rows.']
  is_blocked boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the seat is blocked from assignment due to operational, safety, or commercial reasons.']
  is_complimentary boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the seat assignment was provided at no charge (true) or as a paid ancillary service (false).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this seat assignment record was last updated or modified. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  loyalty_tier_at_assignment varchar [note: 'The passenger\'s loyalty program tier status at the time of seat assignment, which may influence seat selection privileges and complimentary upgrades.']
  previous_seat_number varchar [note: 'The seat number previously assigned to this passenger on this flight segment before a change was made. Null if this is the first assignment.']
  seat_characteristics varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of seat characteristics or attributes such as window, aisle, extra legroom, power outlet, restricted recline, etc.']
  seat_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary amount charged for the seat assignment if it was a paid ancillary product. Zero or null for complimentary assignments.']
  seat_fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the seat assignment fee amount.']
  seat_number varchar [note: 'The alphanumeric seat identifier consisting of row number and seat letter (e.g., 12A, 34F). Follows standard aircraft seat numbering convention.']
  special_service_request_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter IATA SSR code associated with the seat assignment for special needs or requests (e.g., WCHR for wheelchair, PETC for pet in cabin).']
  upgrade_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the type of upgrade applied if the seat assignment represents an upgrade from the originally booked cabin or seat type. Null or none if no upgrade. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paid|complimentary|operational|elite_status|voucher|bid|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a paid or complimentary seat assignment made for a passenger on a specific flight segment. Captures seat number, deck, cabin, seat product type, assignment method (self-select, agent-assigned, auto-assigned), assignment timestamp, associated ancillary order item reference, upgrade type if applicable, and current assignment status. Enables seat revenue tracking and operational manifest generation.'
}

Table ancillary.baggage_allowance {
  baggage_allowance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for baggage_allowance']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: Baggage allowances are defined per cabin class in fare construction rules (business 2 pieces, economy 1 piece). Fare rule application and ticketing require formal cabin class linkage. Fare constructio']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.category. Business justification: Baggage allowances should be classified by ancillary category (e.g., Baggage, Checked Bag, Carry-On). The baggage_allowance table has baggage_category (STRING) but no FK. Adding this FK normalizes the']
  interline_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.interline_agreement. Business justification: Baggage allowances under interline agreements are governed by specific interline agreement terms — IATA Resolution 302 requires carriers to honor partner baggage allowances per interline agreement. Th']
  tier_status_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier_status. Business justification: Elite baggage benefit assignment: baggage allowances are defined per loyalty tier (Platinum gets 3 free bags, Gold gets 2). This FK replaces the denormalized ffp_tier_code text field and enables autom']
  allowance_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the baggage allowance rule, used for reference in fare products and ancillary catalogs.']
  allowance_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the baggage allowance rule (e.g., \'Economy Standard 2PC\', \'Business Premium 3PC 32KG\').']
  allowance_type varchar [note: 'The allowance model applied: piece_concept (number of bags), weight_concept (total weight limit), or hybrid (combination of both).']
  applicable_ptc varchar [note: 'Passenger Type Code this allowance applies to (e.g., ADT=adult, CHD=child, INF=infant, MIL=military). Null if applies to all passenger types.']
  baggage_allowance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the baggage allowance rule: active (in use), inactive (not in use), pending (scheduled for future), expired (past validity), suspended (temporarily disabled).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this baggage allowance record was first created in the system.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the destination of travel. Null if not country-specific.']
  destination_region_code varchar [note: 'IATA region or sub-region code for the destination (e.g., TC1=North America, TC2=Europe, TC3=Asia). Null if not region-specific.']
  dimension_limit_cm varchar [note: 'Maximum dimensions allowed for each piece in centimeters, expressed as LxWxH (e.g., \'158x90x75\'). Null if no dimension restriction applies.']
  dimension_limit_inches varchar [note: 'Maximum dimensions allowed for each piece in inches, expressed as LxWxH (e.g., \'62x35x30\'). Provided for markets using imperial units.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this baggage allowance rule becomes active and applicable to bookings.']
  emd_type varchar [note: 'Type of EMD used to document this baggage allowance when purchased as ancillary: EMD-S (standalone) or EMD-A (associated with e-ticket).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this baggage allowance rule ceases to be valid. Null for open-ended rules.']
  fare_brand_code varchar [note: 'Fare brand or product code associated with this allowance (e.g., BASIC, STANDARD, FLEX, PREMIUM). Null if not brand-specific.']
  is_combinable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allowance can be combined with other allowances (e.g., fare allowance + FFP tier allowance). True if combinable, false if exclusive.']
  is_refundable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the baggage allowance (if purchased as ancillary) is refundable. True if refundable, false if non-refundable.']
  is_transferable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this baggage allowance can be transferred to another passenger or booking. True if transferable, false if non-transferable.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this baggage allowance record was last updated or modified.']
  linear_dimension_limit_cm varchar [note: 'Maximum sum of length, width, and height in centimeters (L+W+H). Common standard is 158 cm for international checked baggage.']
  linear_dimension_limit_inches varchar [note: 'Maximum sum of length, width, and height in inches (L+W+H). Common standard is 62 inches for international checked baggage.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'IATA two-letter airline code of the marketing carrier for which this allowance applies. Null if applies to all carriers.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'IATA two-letter airline code of the operating carrier for which this allowance applies. Null if applies to all carriers.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the origin of travel. Null if not country-specific.']
  origin_region_code varchar [note: 'IATA region or sub-region code for the origin (e.g., TC1=North America, TC2=Europe, TC3=Asia). Null if not region-specific.']
  piece_count varchar [note: 'Number of baggage pieces allowed under this rule (applicable for piece concept allowances). Null if weight concept only.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking used to resolve conflicts when multiple allowance rules apply to the same booking context. Lower number = higher priority.']
  rfisc_code varchar [note: 'IATA RFISC code identifying the specific ancillary service type for baggage (e.g., 0CC1=first checked bag, 0CC2=second checked bag).']
  route_group_code varchar [note: 'Route group or geographic zone code this allowance applies to (e.g., DOM=domestic, INTL=international, TRANSATLANTIC). Null if applies globally.']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text field describing any special conditions, restrictions, or notes applicable to this baggage allowance (e.g., \'Applies only to direct flights\', \'Excludes codeshare partners\').']
  weight_limit_kg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum weight allowed per piece or total weight limit in kilograms. Applicable for weight concept or per-piece weight restrictions.']
  weight_limit_lbs decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum weight allowed per piece or total weight limit in pounds. Provided for markets using imperial units.']

  Note: 'Defines the baggage entitlement rules associated with a fare product, frequent flyer tier, or ancillary purchase — including number of pieces, weight limits (kg/lbs), size restrictions, and applicable route/cabin/PTC combinations. Supports both piece concept and weight concept allowance models per IATA Resolution 302. Used for entitlement validation at check-in and ancillary upsell logic.'
}

Table ancillary.upgrade_offer {
  upgrade_offer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the upgrade offer record. Primary key.']
  aircraft_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Upgrade offer eligibility is restricted by aircraft type in real airline merchandising (e.g., lie-flat upgrades only on widebody aircraft). upgrade_offer.aircraft_type_restriction is a denormalized te']
  minimum_tier_status_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier_status. Business justification: Upgrade offer eligibility enforcement: upgrade offers are restricted to members at or above a minimum loyalty tier (e.g., only Gold+ eligible for bid upgrades). This FK replaces the denormalized ffp_t']
  product_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.product_catalog. Business justification: upgrade_offer defines commercial upgrade offers (e.g., Economy to Business cabin upgrades) which are ancillary revenue products. product_catalog is the master catalog for all ancillary products. Linki']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Revenue management configures upgrade offers per route — long-haul vs. short-haul routes have distinct upgrade pricing and availability. route_restriction is a denormalized text representation of this']
  schedule_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.schedule_season. Business justification: Upgrade offer campaigns are planned and activated per IATA schedule season — airlines launch seasonal upgrade promotions aligned to schedule season cycles. upgrade_offer has effective_start_date/end_d']
  source_cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: Upgrade offers specify source cabin class for eligibility (economy to business, business to first). Upgrade program design and offer targeting require formal cabin class linkage for source eligibility']
  channel_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of distribution channels where this upgrade offer is available (e.g., web, mobile app, airport kiosk, call center, Global Distribution System (GDS)). Defines merchandising channel strategy.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this upgrade offer record. Audit field for accountability and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this upgrade offer record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this offer (minimum bid, maximum bid, fixed price).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this upgrade offer expires and is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended offer with no expiration. Defines the end of the offers lifecycle.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this upgrade offer becomes valid and available for presentation to passengers. Defines the beginning of the offers lifecycle.']
  eligible_fare_classes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of fare class codes (booking classes) that qualify for this upgrade offer. Fare classes determine revenue management eligibility and upgrade priority.']
  emd_type_code varchar [note: 'IATA Electronic Miscellaneous Document (EMD) type code associated with this upgrade offer when issued as an ancillary charge. Typically \'A\' for EMD-A (standalone) or \'S\' for EMD-S (associated with e-ticket).']
  fixed_price_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The fixed monetary price for instant upgrade offers. Null for bid-based or miles-based offers. Represents the non-negotiable purchase price.']
  inventory_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this upgrade offer is subject to real-time inventory availability checks in the target cabin. True means offer availability depends on seat inventory; false means offer is always available regardless of inventory.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this upgrade offer record. Audit field for change accountability and compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this upgrade offer record was last updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  maximum_bid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The maximum monetary bid amount allowed for bid-based upgrade offers. Null for non-bid offer types. Represents the ceiling price for competitive bidding.']
  miles_required varchar [note: 'Number of Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) miles required to redeem this upgrade offer. Null for non-miles-based offers.']
  minimum_bid_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The minimum monetary bid amount required for bid-based upgrade offers. Null for non-bid offer types. Represents the floor price for competitive bidding.']
  offer_close_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before scheduled departure when this upgrade offer closes and is no longer available for purchase or bidding. Defines the closing of the offer window.']
  offer_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code identifying this upgrade offer in merchandising and revenue management systems.']
  offer_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the upgrade offer including benefits, terms, and conditions presented to passengers during the offer flow.']
  offer_name varchar [note: 'Marketing name or title of the upgrade offer displayed to passengers in booking channels and merchandising platforms.']
  offer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the upgrade offer. Active offers are available for purchase or bidding; inactive offers are not currently presented; expired offers have passed their validity window; suspended offers are temporarily paused; withdrawn offers have been permanently removed.']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the upgrade offer mechanism. Bid upgrade allows passengers to submit competitive bids; instant upgrade is fixed-price immediate purchase; miles upgrade uses Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) miles; operational upgrade is airline-initiated for operational reasons; points upgrade uses loyalty points; voucher upgrade redeems a voucher or certificate.']
  offer_window_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before scheduled departure that this upgrade offer becomes available to eligible passengers. Defines the opening of the offer window.']
  points_required varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty points required to redeem this upgrade offer. Null for non-points-based offers. Distinct from miles for coalition or partner loyalty programs.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for this upgrade offer when multiple offers are available. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for offer sequencing and conflict resolution.']
  revenue_accounting_code varchar [note: 'Internal accounting code used to classify and track revenue generated from this upgrade offer in financial reporting and revenue management systems.']
  target_cabin_class varchar [note: 'The destination cabin class to which the passenger will be upgraded if the offer is accepted and confirmed.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'Web URL linking to the full legal terms and conditions governing this upgrade offer. Required for consumer protection and transparency compliance.']

  Note: 'Defines commercial upgrade offers presented to passengers for cabin upgrades (e.g., Economy to Business) via bid, fixed-price, or miles-based mechanisms. Captures offer type (bid upgrade, instant upgrade, miles upgrade, operational upgrade), eligible fare classes, minimum and maximum bid amounts, offer window (hours before departure), target cabin, and channel availability. Supports revenue-optimized upgrade merchandising programs.'
}

Table ancillary.upgrade_transaction {
  upgrade_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for upgrade_transaction']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Paid upgrade transactions (cash bids, miles+cash) create AR entries for collection tracking. Aviation finance requires AR linkage on upgrade transactions for payment reconciliation, dunning, and BSP s']
  ancillary_emd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.ancillary_emd. Business justification: Paid upgrade transactions are documented via EMD. The upgrade_transaction table has emd_number (STRING) but no FK. Adding this FK links upgrade transactions to EMD documentation for revenue accounting']
  booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.booking_passenger. Business justification: Associates upgrade transactions with booking passengers for passenger-specific upgrade tracking, bid management, and service delivery. Required for upgrade program passenger attribution.']
  checkin_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.checkin_session. Business justification: Operational upgrades are frequently processed at check-in counters. Linking upgrade_transaction to checkin_session enables upgrade audit trails tied to specific check-in events, supports IROPS upgrade']
  destination_fare_class_bucket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.fare_class_bucket. Business justification: Upgrade clearance tracking by destination fare class bucket is a core RM process — upgrades consume inventory in the destination RBD bucket. upgrade_transaction has destination_fare_class as a plain d']
  e_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.e_ticket. Business justification: Paid or bid upgrades require ticket reissuance or EMD generation tied to a specific e-ticket. Revenue accounting and audit trails for upgrade transactions must reference the resulting/associated e-tic']
  ffp_member_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty program member account used for miles redemption or status-based upgrade eligibility.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Paid upgrades (bid_amount, upgrade_amount) generate revenue postings to GL for IFRS 15 recognition. Aviation revenue accounting teams post upgrade revenue separately from base fare; direct GL link on ']
  inventory_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_leg. Business justification: Upgrade transactions affect inventory capacity on specific legs — the destination cabin gains a booking while the origin cabin releases one. Linking upgrade_transaction to inventory_leg enables RM sys']
  itinerary_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.itinerary_segment. Business justification: Associates upgrade transactions with flight segments for segment-specific upgrade tracking, inventory control, and operational reporting. Required for per-segment upgrade management.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the passenger who received or purchased the upgrade.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.pnr. Business justification: Associates upgrade transactions with booking for upgrade revenue tracking, bid management, and passenger service history. Essential for upgrade program reporting and revenue optimization.']
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the scheduled flight on which the upgrade was applied.']
  upgrade_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.upgrade_offer. Business justification: Upgrade transactions are based on specific upgrade offers (e.g., bid upgrade, mileage upgrade, paid upgrade). No FK currently exists. Adding this FK links transactions to their originating offer defin']
  bid_acceptance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the upgrade bid was accepted and confirmed by the system. Null for non-bid or unsuccessful bid upgrades.']
  bid_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount bid by the passenger in an upgrade auction or bid program. Null for non-bid upgrades.']
  bid_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the passenger submitted the upgrade bid. Null for non-bid upgrades.']
  booking_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the upgrade transaction was initiated: web (airline website), mobile_app (airline mobile application), call_center (phone reservation), airport_kiosk (self-service kiosk), gds (Global Distribution System), travel_agent (third-party agent), partner (codeshare or alliance partner). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|call_center|airport_kiosk|gds|travel_agent|partner — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the upgrade transaction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the upgrade transaction amount.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining why the upgrade was denied: insufficient_inventory (no seats available), low_priority (other passengers cleared first), payment_failed (transaction declined), ineligible_fare (fare class not eligible), system_error (technical issue), expired (offer expired), cancelled_by_pax (passenger cancelled). Null if upgrade not denied. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insufficient_inventory|low_priority|payment_failed|ineligible_fare|system_error|expired|cancelled_by_pax — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  destination_cabin_class varchar [note: 'The target cabin class to which the passenger is upgrading (economy, premium_economy, business, first).']
  destination_fare_class varchar [note: 'Single-letter fare class code assigned after the upgrade is confirmed.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the upgrade transaction record was last updated, reflecting status changes or data corrections.']
  miles_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of frequent flyer miles consumed for the upgrade. Zero for non-mileage upgrades.']
  origin_cabin_class varchar [note: 'The original cabin class from which the passenger is upgrading (economy, premium_economy, business, first).']
  origin_fare_class varchar [note: 'Single-letter fare class code of the original booking, determining upgrade eligibility and priority.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used for the upgrade transaction: credit_card, debit_card, miles (FFP miles), voucher (upgrade certificate), cash, bank_transfer, none (complimentary or operational). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|miles|voucher|cash|bank_transfer|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_score varchar [note: 'Calculated priority score determining upgrade clearance order, based on fare class, elite status, bid amount, and other factors.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount refunded to the passenger if the upgrade was cancelled or denied after payment, in the transaction currency. Zero if no refund issued.']
  refund_processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was processed and returned to the passenger. Null if no refund issued.']
  seat_number_destination varchar [note: 'New seat assignment after the upgrade is cleared (e.g., 2A, 8D).']
  seat_number_origin varchar [note: 'Original seat assignment before the upgrade (e.g., 23A, 45F).']
  upgrade_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount paid by the passenger for the upgrade, in the transaction currency. Zero for non-purchased upgrades (miles, operational, complimentary).']
  upgrade_cleared_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the upgrade was fully processed, seat assigned, and boarding pass updated.']
  upgrade_confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the upgrade was confirmed and guaranteed to the passenger.']
  upgrade_request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the upgrade transaction was initiated or requested by the passenger or system.']
  upgrade_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the upgrade transaction: pending (submitted awaiting processing), confirmed (upgrade guaranteed), waitlisted (on standby list), cleared (upgrade processed and seat assigned), denied (upgrade request rejected), cancelled (customer or system cancelled), expired (upgrade offer or bid expired). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|confirmed|waitlisted|cleared|denied|cancelled|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  upgrade_transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique transaction number for the upgrade event, used for customer service reference and audit tracking.']
  upgrade_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the upgrade method: purchased (paid cash upgrade), bid_won (successful upgrade bid), miles_redeemed (FFP miles used), operational (involuntary operational upgrade), complimentary (airline-initiated goodwill), status_based (elite status automatic upgrade), voucher (upgrade certificate or voucher applied). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchased|bid_won|miles_redeemed|operational|complimentary|status_based|voucher — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  voucher_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code of the upgrade voucher or certificate applied to this transaction. Null if no voucher used.']
  waitlist_position varchar [note: 'Passenger\'s position on the upgrade waitlist at the time of transaction creation. Null if not waitlisted.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a passenger cabin upgrade event, whether purchased, bid-won, miles-redeemed, or operationally assigned. Captures upgrade type, origin cabin, destination cabin, amount paid or miles consumed, bid submission and acceptance timestamps, flight segment, passenger reference, and upgrade status (pending, confirmed, waitlisted, cleared, denied). Enables upgrade revenue tracking and yield impact analysis.'
}

Table ancillary.eligibility_rule {
  eligibility_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for eligibility_rule']
  cabin_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.cabin_configuration. Business justification: Ancillary eligibility rules must be scoped to specific cabin configurations when airlines retrofit aircraft (e.g., new premium economy cabin). Rules like \'seat selection product X eligible only on con']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ancillary.ancillary_category. Business justification: eligibility_rule defines business rules governing which passengers, fare classes, routes, and booking windows can access ancillary products. While eligibility_rule already links to product_catalog for']
  mel_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to maintenance.mel_item. Business justification: Ancillary eligibility rules must suppress product sales (wifi, IFE, specific seat features) when the corresponding equipment is on MEL. Revenue management teams create eligibility rules referencing th']
  product_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific ancillary product or service to which this eligibility rule applies.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Eligibility rules restrict ancillary availability by specific routes (e.g., premium seats only on long-haul, lounge access on international). Real business process: merchandising rules engine evaluate']
  schedule_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.schedule_season. Business justification: Ancillary product eligibility rules are configured per IATA schedule season — winter season rules differ from summer (e.g., ski equipment, seasonal fare class eligibility). Revenue management reviews ']
  aircraft_type_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA aircraft type codes (e.g., 737, 777, A320) for which this ancillary product is eligible. Empty indicates no aircraft restriction.']
  booking_date_from date [note: 'Start date of the booking window during which this eligibility rule is active. Null indicates no booking start date restriction.']
  booking_date_to date [note: 'End date of the booking window during which this eligibility rule is active. Null indicates no booking end date restriction.']
  cabin_class_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of cabin classes (First, Business, Premium Economy, Economy) eligible for this ancillary product. Empty indicates no cabin restriction.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this eligibility rule record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this eligibility rule record was first created in the system.']
  destination_airport_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA airport codes representing eligible destination points. Empty indicates no destination restriction.']
  destination_country_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes representing eligible destination countries. Empty indicates no destination country restriction.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this eligibility rule becomes active and enforceable in all sales channels.']
  exclusion_rule_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is an exclusion rule (true) that prevents eligibility, or an inclusion rule (false) that grants eligibility.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this eligibility rule is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended rule.']
  fare_class_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of booking class codes (RBD - Revenue Booking Designator) to which this rule applies. Empty indicates no fare class restriction.']
  ffp_tier_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of loyalty program tier codes (e.g., Gold, Platinum, Diamond) eligible for this ancillary product. Empty indicates no tier restriction.']
  is_combinable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this eligibility rule can be combined with other rules (true) or must be evaluated independently (false).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that most recently modified this eligibility rule record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this eligibility rule record was most recently updated.']
  marketing_carrier_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA airline codes representing marketing carriers for which this rule applies. Empty indicates all carriers.']
  maximum_advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days before departure that the ancillary product may be purchased. Null indicates no maximum advance purchase restriction.']
  minimum_advance_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days before departure that the ancillary product must be purchased. Null indicates no minimum advance purchase requirement.']
  operating_carrier_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA airline codes representing operating carriers for which this rule applies. Empty indicates all carriers.']
  origin_airport_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA airport codes representing eligible origin points. Empty indicates no origin restriction.']
  origin_country_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes representing eligible origin countries. Empty indicates no origin country restriction.']
  override_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether authorized agents or systems may override this eligibility rule under exceptional circumstances.']
  passenger_type_code_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA passenger type codes (ADT, CHD, INF, MIL, SRC, etc.) eligible for this ancillary product. Empty indicates no passenger type restriction.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority order for rule evaluation when multiple rules apply to the same ancillary product. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code identifying this eligibility rule for operational reference and system integration.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the eligibility rule, including rationale, business context, and any special conditions or exceptions.']
  rule_logic_expression varchar [note: 'Complex logical expression or formula defining advanced eligibility conditions beyond standard attribute filters. Used for multi-condition rules requiring AND/OR/NOT logic.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the purpose and scope of this eligibility rule.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the eligibility rule indicating whether it is actively enforced in sales channels.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the eligibility rule defining the primary dimension of restriction or entitlement (fare class, route, FFP tier, booking window, passenger type, or cabin).']
  sales_channel_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sales channels (web, mobile, GDS, call_center, airport, agency) through which this ancillary product may be purchased. Empty indicates all channels.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'Web address linking to the full terms and conditions document governing this eligibility rule for customer transparency and regulatory compliance.']
  travel_date_from date [note: 'Start date of the travel period during which this eligibility rule applies. Null indicates no start date restriction.']
  travel_date_to date [note: 'End date of the travel period during which this eligibility rule applies. Null indicates no end date restriction.']

  Note: 'Business rules defining which passengers, fare classes, routes, cabin types, booking windows, and FFP tiers are eligible to purchase specific ancillary products. Captures rule type (fare-class restriction, route restriction, tier-based entitlement, advance-purchase window, passenger-type restriction), effective date range, priority order, and override flags. Drives real-time ancillary offer eligibility checks across all sales channels.'
}

Table inventory.flight_inventory {
  flight_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the flight inventory record representing the sellable seat inventory for a specific flight-date combination in the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system.']
  aircraft_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft. Business justification: Flight inventory is opened on a specific tail number for each departure. Operations Control Center assigns aircraft to flights; inventory management needs tail number for MEL item applicability, actua']
  cabin_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.cabin_configuration. Business justification: When equipment changes occur (equipment_change_indicator on flight_inventory), the inventory system must reference the active cabin configuration to recompute class capacities (business_class_capacity']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Flight operations are cost centers in airline accounting. CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) calculations require linking flight inventory to cost centers for profitability analysis and manageme']
  fleet_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route_fleet_assignment. Business justification: Fleet assignment determines aircraft type, seat configuration, and cabin class capacity that inventory must control. Revenue management bucket authorization, overbooking limits, and codeshare allocati']
  flight_number_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.flight_number. Business justification: Flight inventory controls real-time seat availability for specific flight numbers defined in route planning. Revenue management, codeshare allocation, and GDS distribution all require linking daily in']
  origin_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Flight inventory capacity records must link to origin station for slot-coordinated market compliance reporting, station-level load factor dashboards, and hub capacity planning. Aviation revenue manage']
  route_id varchar [note: 'FK to route.route.route_id — Flight inventory must reference the route for network-level availability queries, codeshare allocation management, and seasonal capacity planning.']
  scheduled_flight_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled flight leg for which this inventory record is maintained. Links to the flight master entity in the flight operations domain.']
  seasonal_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.seasonal_schedule. Business justification: Flight inventory operationalizes a specific seasonal schedule template. Linking inventory to seasonal_schedule enables variance analysis (planned vs actual capacity), schedule change impact assessment']
  altea_inventory_key varchar [note: 'Native primary key or reference identifier assigned by the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system to this flight inventory record. Used for system-of-record traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational PSS.']
  authorized_capacity varchar [note: 'Revenue management-approved seat count authorized for sale on this flight-date, which may differ from total physical capacity due to operational holds, crew seat requirements, or regulatory restrictions. This is the effective inventory ceiling used by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management.']
  availability_snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent real-time availability snapshot broadcast to GDS and direct booking channels. Reflects the point-in-time at which seats_available and fare class bucket statuses were last synchronized with Amadeus Altéa.']
  booking_curve_day varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to departure at which this inventory snapshot was captured, representing the position on the booking curve (e.g., 90, 60, 30, 14, 7, 1). Used by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management for demand forecasting and fare class optimization.']
  business_class_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of seats allocated to the Business Class cabin on this flight-date. Reflects the aircraft configuration and any cabin reconfiguration applied for this specific flight.']
  channel_restriction_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any channel-specific sales restrictions applied to this flight inventory (e.g., \'DIRECT_ONLY\' for web/app exclusive fares, \'GDS_BLOCKED\' for GDS channel closure, \'NDC_ONLY\' for IATA NDC channel exclusivity). Controls distribution channel access to inventory.']
  codeshare_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this flight inventory record includes codeshare seat allocations from partner airlines. When true, a portion of the authorized capacity is reserved for codeshare partner bookings under interline ticketing agreements.']
  codeshare_seats_allocated varchar [note: 'Number of seats within the authorized capacity ring-fenced for codeshare partner airline bookings on this flight-date. Managed under bilateral codeshare agreements and tracked separately from own-metal inventory for revenue accounting and BSP settlement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this flight inventory record was first created in the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system, typically at the time of schedule publication or inventory initialization for the flight-date.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled local departure date of the flight leg at the origin station. Together with flight_number, forms the primary business key for inventory lookup by GDS and booking channels.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival station of this flight leg (e.g., LAX, CDG). Used to identify the inventory leg destination in route network and GDS availability queries.']
  economy_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of seats allocated to the Economy Class cabin on this flight-date. Typically the largest cabin and primary driver of load factor and revenue management decisions.']
