  gds_availability_code varchar [note: 'Availability status code published to Global Distribution Systems (GDS) such as Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport for this segment and booking class. Standard codes include numeric seat counts (0-9) or alpha codes (A=available, C=closed, L=waitlist) per IATA AIRIMP standards.']
  group_seats_held varchar [note: 'Number of seats currently held under group booking reservations for this segment. Group holds reduce available inventory and are subject to release deadlines managed by the group desk.']
  interline_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory segment is eligible for interline ticketing agreements, allowing passengers to book connecting itineraries across multiple carriers on a single ticket. Governed by bilateral interline agreements and IATA BSP/ARC settlement rules.']
  load_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current load factor for this inventory segment expressed as a percentage of physical cabin capacity that is sold or confirmed (seats_sold / physical_capacity * 100). Key performance indicator for revenue management and capacity planning. Note: this is a point-in-time snapshot value, not a computed aggregate.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier that markets and sells this segment to passengers. For codeshare flights, this is the airline whose flight number appears on the ticket.']
  min_connecting_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum Connecting Time (MCT) in minutes required at the connecting airport for this segment to be valid in a connecting itinerary. Enforced during availability display and booking to prevent infeasible connections. Sourced from IATA SSIM MCT tables.']
  nesting_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical nesting level of this booking class within the cabin\'s availability nesting structure (e.g., 1=highest yield class, 10=lowest yield class). Governs through-availability rules: higher-level classes inherit availability from lower-level buckets per revenue management nesting policy.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier that physically operates the aircraft for this segment. Differs from marketing_carrier_code on codeshare flights.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the origin station of this commercial O&D segment as sold to the passenger. Defines the departure point of the sellable unit.']
  overbooking_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of seats that may be sold beyond the physical cabin capacity for this segment, as authorized by revenue management overbooking controls. Expressed as an absolute seat count above physical capacity.']
  overbooking_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overbooking authorization expressed as a percentage above physical cabin capacity (e.g., 5.00 = 5% overbooking). Used by revenue management to calibrate no-show risk models and optimize load factor.']
  sale_close_date date [note: 'Date after which this inventory segment is no longer available for new bookings. Typically set to the departure date or earlier based on advance purchase restrictions and revenue management close-out rules.']
  sale_open_date date [note: 'Date from which this inventory segment is open for sale in GDS and direct booking channels. Defines the advance purchase window start for revenue management and distribution control.']
  scheduled_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled arrival date and time of the segment at the destination airport in UTC (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for MCT enforcement and connecting itinerary feasibility validation.']
  scheduled_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled departure date and time of the segment at the origin airport in UTC (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for precise GDS availability display and MCT (Minimum Connecting Time) enforcement calculations.']
  seats_available varchar [note: 'Current count of seats available for sale on this segment, computed as authorized_capacity minus seats_sold minus seats_blocked. This is the real-time availability figure published to GDS and direct channels.']
  seats_blocked varchar [note: 'Number of seats held back from general sale for operational purposes such as crew positioning, group holds, codeshare allocations, or special service requests (SSR). Reduces net available inventory without being sold.']
  seats_sold varchar [note: 'Number of seats currently confirmed as sold (booked and ticketed) on this inventory segment. Updated in real-time from the Passenger Service System (PSS) as bookings are made or cancelled.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current availability status of this inventory segment as published to GDS and direct booking channels. \'open\' = seats available for sale; \'closed\' = no availability; \'waitlisted\' = demand exceeds supply, waitlist active; \'cancelled\' = flight/segment cancelled; \'suspended\' = temporarily removed from sale.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the commercial segment by its operational and commercial structure: \'direct\' = single leg O&D; \'connecting\' = multi-leg through-availability segment; \'codeshare\' = marketed by partner airline; \'interline\' = cross-carrier ticketing agreement; \'virtual\' = constructed availability for display purposes.']
  through_availability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment participates in through-availability nesting, meaning its availability is derived from the aggregated availability of underlying leg buckets rather than being independently controlled. True = through-availability applies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp (UTC) of the most recent update to this inventory segment record, including availability changes, overbooking adjustments, or status transitions. Used for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  waitlist_count varchar [note: 'Number of passengers currently on the waitlist for this inventory segment and booking class. Used by revenue management to assess latent demand and inform overbooking and upgrade decisions.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a commercial O&D (origin-destination) segment as sold to passengers, which may span one or more physical inventory legs. Captures the marketed flight number, O&D city pair, cabin, fare basis, and the through-availability nesting rules that govern how segment availability is derived from underlying leg buckets. Critical for connecting-itinerary inventory control and MCT (Minimum Connecting Time) enforcement.'
}

Table inventory.group_block {
  group_block_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the group block record in the inventory system. Primary key for this entity.']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Group blocks carry deposit_amount, deposit_paid, deposit_paid_date, and final_payment_due_date — all generating AR records. Finance tracks group booking deposits and final payments as receivables. Rev']
  cabin_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: group_block allocates seats in a specific cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links group block records to the cabin master. cabin_code is redundant and ava']
  fare_class_bucket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.fare_class_bucket. Business justification: group_block carries a denormalized `booking_class_code` STRING column representing the RBD code for the group allocation. fare_class_bucket is the authoritative master entity for booking class buckets']
  fare_class_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fare class bucket (booking class) under which the group block seats are held. Determines the yield and revenue management bucket impacted by this block.']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: group_block reserves a seat block for a group booking on a specific flight-date. flight_inventory is the master inventory record for that flight-date. This FK establishes the natural parent-child rela']
  inventory_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_leg. Business justification: group_block currently links to flight.flight_leg (cross-domain operational leg) but lacks a link to inventory.inventory_leg (the inventory management unit). For multi-leg itineraries, group blocks mus']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master Passenger Name Record (PNR) associated with this group block. A group block is linked to a single group PNR in the Passenger Service System (PSS).']
  block_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the group block. \'tentative\' = seats held pending confirmation; \'confirmed\' = group has committed; \'ticketed\' = tickets issued; \'cancelled\' = block released back to inventory; \'expired\' = option date passed without confirmation; \'waitlisted\' = requested but not yet allocated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tentative|confirmed|ticketed|cancelled|expired|waitlisted — promote to reference product]']
  codeshare_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this group block is associated with a codeshare flight operation, where the operating carrier differs from the marketing carrier. True = codeshare flight; False = own-metal operation. Affects inventory allocation and interline settlement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this group block record was first created in the inventory system. Supports audit trail, SLA tracking, and data lineage requirements.']
  deposit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'The monetary deposit amount required or received for this group block, expressed in fare_currency_code. Null if deposit_required is False.']
  deposit_paid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required deposit has been received from the group organizer. True = deposit received; False = deposit outstanding. Drives follow-up and cancellation workflows.']
  deposit_paid_date date [note: 'The date on which the deposit payment was received from the group organizer. Null if deposit has not been paid.']
  deposit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a deposit payment is required from the group organizer to hold or confirm this group block. True = deposit required; False = no deposit required.']
  fare_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the group_fare_amount is denominated (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  final_payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the full fare payment for all ticketed seats in the group block must be settled. Distinct from the deposit due date and ticketing deadline.']
  free_seat_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'The ratio of complimentary (free) seats granted to the group organizer relative to the total seats purchased, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1000 = 1 free seat per 10 paid). Drives revenue accounting and yield calculations.']
  free_seats_allocated varchar [note: 'The actual number of complimentary (free) seats allocated to the group organizer based on the free_seat_ratio and confirmed group size. Impacts revenue accounting and load factor reporting.']
  group_fare_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Negotiated per-seat fare amount agreed for this group block, expressed in the currency specified by fare_currency_code. Represents the contracted price per passenger for revenue accounting and settlement.']
  group_fare_basis varchar [note: 'The fare basis code applicable to the group block, defining the fare rules, restrictions, and pricing conditions under which the group seats are sold (e.g., \'GEXCAP\', \'YGROUP\'). Aligns with IATA fare construction rules.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'The commercial or organizational name of the group for whom the seat block is reserved (e.g., \'Smith Wedding Party\', \'ACME Corp Sales Conference\'). Used for identification and communication with the group organizer.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the group by purpose or segment. Used for revenue management analysis, pricing strategy, and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: leisure|corporate|incentive|military|religious|sports|charter — promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this group block record was most recently updated in the inventory system. Used for change detection, ETL delta processing, and audit compliance.']
  minimum_group_size varchar [note: 'The minimum number of passengers required to qualify for group fare pricing on this block, as defined by the applicable fare rules. Typically 10 passengers per IATA group fare conventions.']
  names_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the group organizer must submit individual passenger names (Pax names) for all seats in the confirmed block. Required for check-in, security compliance, and advance passenger information (API) submission.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier actually operating the flight (relevant for codeshare blocks where the operating carrier may differ from the marketing carrier). Required for interline settlement and ACARS reporting.']
  option_date date [note: 'The deadline date by which the group organizer must confirm the number of seats and provide passenger names. If not confirmed by this date, the block may be automatically released back to inventory. Critical for inventory management and revenue optimization.']
  overbooking_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this group block is subject to overbooking controls. True = the block counts against overbooking limits; False = the block is protected from overbooking displacement (e.g., guaranteed allocation). Feeds revenue management overbooking models.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this group block by the Passenger Service System (PSS) or reservations team. Used by travel agents and group coordinators to identify and manage the block.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or special instructions associated with this group block, entered by the reservations agent or group coordinator. May include special service requests (SSR), handling notes, or commercial conditions.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel through which the group block was requested and booked. \'direct\' = airline reservations team; \'gds\' = Global Distribution System (GDS) such as Amadeus or Sabre; \'online_travel_agency\' = OTA; \'corporate\' = corporate travel desk; \'charter\' = charter operator.']
  seats_blocked varchar [note: 'Current number of seats held (blocked) in inventory for this group. Reduces available seat count for individual sale. May differ from seats_requested if the airline partially approved the block.']
  seats_confirmed varchar [note: 'Number of seats within the block that have been formally confirmed by the group organizer. May be less than seats_blocked during the tentative phase. Drives revenue recognition and ticketing obligations.']
  seats_released varchar [note: 'Number of seats that have been released back to general inventory from this group block, either due to partial cancellation, reduction, or expiry of the option. Supports inventory reconciliation.']
  seats_requested varchar [note: 'Total number of seats originally requested by the group organizer at the time of the initial group block request. Baseline for tracking changes to the block size.']
  seats_ticketed varchar [note: 'Number of seats within the confirmed block for which electronic tickets (e-tickets) have been issued. Used to track ticketing progress against the ticketing deadline.']
  ticketing_deadline date [note: 'The final date by which all confirmed seats in the group block must be ticketed (e-tickets issued). Failure to ticket by this date may result in automatic cancellation of unticketed seats and release to general inventory.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a reserved seat block allocated to a group booking request on a specific flight-date and cabin. Captures the group name, number of seats blocked, option date (deadline for confirmation), ticketing deadline, applicable fare class, deposit status, and the linked PNR reference. Group blocks reduce available inventory for individual sale and must be tracked separately from regular fare class bucket availability.'
}

Table inventory.waitlist_entry {
  waitlist_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each waitlist entry record in the Altéa Inventory waitlist processing engine. Primary key for this data product.']
  booking_passenger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_booking_passenger. Business justification: Waitlist clearance workflow requires identifying the specific passenger (not just PNR) to notify, upgrade, and trigger boarding pass issuance. A PNR can have multiple passengers; without this FK, the ']
  fare_class_bucket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific fare class booking class bucket (e.g., Y, B, M, Q) for which availability was closed and the waitlist entry was created. Drives revenue management yield optimization.']
  fare_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to revenue.fare_class. Business justification: Waitlist entries specify the requested fare class (RBD) for clearance processing. Waitlist clearance algorithms prioritize by fare class yield, eligibility rules, and bucket authorization. Essential f']
  flight_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific flight inventory record (flight-date-cabin combination) for which this waitlist entry is held. Identifies the sellable inventory unit in Altéa Inventory.']
  group_block_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the group booking record when the waitlist entry originates from a group overflow scenario (waitlist_reason = GROUP_OVERFLOW). Links the individual waitlist entry back to the parent group contract for group management reporting.']
  pax_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual passenger profile associated with this waitlist entry. A single PNR may contain multiple passengers, each generating a separate waitlist entry.']
  pnr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Passenger Name Record (PNR) booking that is placed on the waitlist. Links the waitlist entry to the originating reservation in the Passenger Service System (PSS).']
  altea_waitlist_sequence varchar [note: 'Native waitlist sequence number assigned by the Amadeus Altéa Inventory waitlist processing engine. Serves as the business identifier for this waitlist entry within the Altéa system, used for reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the source PSS.']
  booking_class varchar [note: 'Single-letter IATA booking class (fare class) code within the requested cabin for which the waitlist entry was created (e.g., Y, B, M, H, Q, K, L, V, W, S, N, G). Directly maps to a revenue management fare class bucket in Altéa Inventory.']
  cabin_code varchar [note: 'IATA single-character cabin designator for the cabin class requested by the waitlisted passenger. F: First Class; C: Business Class; W: Premium Economy; Y: Economy. Determines the inventory pool from which clearance will be attempted.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Distribution channel through which the waitlist entry was created. GDS: Global Distribution System (e.g., Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport); NDC: IATA New Distribution Capability channel; DIRECT_WEB: airline direct website; MOBILE: airline mobile application; AIRPORT: airport check-in or gate agent; CALL_CENTER: reservations call center; INTERLINE: interline partner booking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GDS|NDC|DIRECT_WEB|MOBILE|AIRPORT|CALL_CENTER|INTERLINE — promote to reference product]']
  clearance_booking_class varchar [note: 'The actual booking class in which the waitlist entry was cleared and confirmed, which may differ from the originally requested booking class if the revenue management system cleared the passenger into a higher-availability bucket. Null until clearance occurs.']
  clearance_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the waitlist entry was cleared. AUTOMATIC: Altéa Inventory engine cleared automatically when bucket availability opened; MANUAL: airport or reservations agent manually cleared the entry; UPGRADE_PROCESSING: cleared as part of an upgrade processing run; IROP_REACCOMMODATION: cleared during Irregular Operations (IROP) reaccommodation workflow.']
  clearance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the waitlist entry was successfully cleared and the seat confirmed. Null if the entry has not yet been cleared. Used to measure waitlist clearance latency and revenue recovery timing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the waitlist entry was first created in the Altéa Inventory system. Serves as the business event timestamp for queue ordering and audit purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival airport of the waitlisted flight segment (e.g., LAX, CDG, SIN). Used for route-level waitlist analysis and GDS availability messaging.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) at which the waitlist entry will automatically expire if not cleared, typically aligned with the flight departure time or a configurable pre-departure deadline set by revenue management policy. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  fare_basis_code varchar [note: 'IATA fare basis code associated with the waitlisted booking (e.g., YLOWUS, BAPUS21). Encodes the fare family, booking class, and applicable restrictions. Used by revenue management to validate clearance eligibility and by pricing to re-price upon confirmation.']
  ffp_member_number varchar [note: 'Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) membership number of the waitlisted passenger. Used to apply loyalty-based priority rules and to credit miles upon clearance and travel. Classified as confidential PII as it is a unique personal identifier.']
  ffp_tier varchar [note: 'Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) membership tier of the waitlisted passenger at the time of waitlist entry creation (e.g., NONE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM, ELITE). Higher tiers receive elevated priority rank in the waitlist queue per revenue management policy.']
  flight_date date [note: 'Scheduled local departure date of the flight for which the waitlist entry is held. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Used for inventory snapshot reporting and waitlist aging analysis.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'IATA flight number (carrier designator + numeric suffix, e.g., AA101) for the flight on which the waitlist entry is held. Denormalized from the flight inventory record for operational reporting and GDS availability messaging.']
  gds_queue_number varchar [note: 'Queue number assigned by the Global Distribution System (GDS) for this waitlist entry, used to route clearance notifications back to the originating travel agent or booking system. Null for direct-channel bookings.']
  is_codeshare boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the waitlist entry is associated with a codeshare flight operation where the marketing carrier differs from the operating carrier. True: codeshare booking; False: own-metal operation. Affects inventory allotment rules and clearance authority.']
  marketing_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier under whose flight number the ticket was sold (marketing carrier). For codeshare flights, this may differ from the operating carrier. Determines which inventory pool and allotment rules apply.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a clearance or status-change notification has been dispatched to the passenger or booking agent following a waitlist status transition. True: notification sent; False: notification pending or not applicable. Supports DOT consumer notification compliance.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the clearance or status-change notification was dispatched to the passenger or booking agent. Null if no notification has been sent. Used for DOT consumer protection compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  operating_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier actually operating the flight (e.g., AA, BA, EK). Relevant for codeshare flights where the marketing carrier differs from the operating carrier.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure airport of the waitlisted flight segment (e.g., JFK, LHR, DXB). Used for route-level waitlist analysis and GDS availability messaging.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank of this waitlist entry within the queue for the same flight, cabin, and fare class bucket. Lower values indicate higher priority for clearance. Determined by Altéa Inventory based on booking time, fare paid, and frequent flyer tier.']
  revenue_management_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a revenue management analyst manually overrode the standard Altéa Inventory waitlist clearance rules for this entry (e.g., to prioritise a high-value passenger or honour a corporate contract). True: manual override applied; False: standard automated rules applied.']
  seat_count_requested varchar [note: 'Number of seats requested on the waitlist for this entry. For individual bookings this is typically 1; for group bookings this may be greater than 1. Determines the inventory units required for clearance.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this waitlist entry. Primarily ALTEA_INV (Amadeus Altéa Inventory) but may include GDS source codes for externally originated waitlist requests. Used for data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver layer.']
  ssr_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA Special Service Request (SSR) codes associated with the waitlisted passenger (e.g., WCHR, VGML, UMNR). These SSRs must be honoured upon clearance and seat assignment. Stored as a denormalized string for operational reference.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: '13-digit IATA electronic ticket (e-ticket) number associated with the waitlisted booking segment. Follows the IATA 13-digit format (3-digit airline code + 10-digit serial). May be null if the ticket has not yet been issued at the time of waitlist creation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (UTC) when the waitlist entry record was last modified in the source system, including status transitions, priority re-ranking, or clearance processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  upgrade_from_cabin_code varchar [note: 'For upgrade-request waitlist entries, the cabin code of the originally booked cabin from which the passenger is requesting an upgrade (e.g., Y for Economy). Null for sold-out or group overflow waitlist reasons. Used by upgrade processing logic in Altéa Inventory.']
  waitlist_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason why the passenger or booking was placed on the waitlist rather than confirmed. SOLD_OUT: requested fare class bucket was closed; UPGRADE_REQUEST: passenger requested cabin upgrade pending availability; GROUP_OVERFLOW: group booking exceeded available allotment; OVERBOOKING_CONTROL: revenue management overbooking limit reached; CODESHARE_ALLOTMENT: codeshare partner inventory allocation exhausted.']
  waitlist_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the waitlist entry as managed by the Altéa Inventory waitlist processing engine. OPEN: actively queued awaiting availability; CLEARED: bucket opened and seat confirmed; CANCELLED: passenger or agent cancelled the waitlist request; EXPIRED: flight departed or deadline passed without clearance; REJECTED: system or revenue management rule prevented clearance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a passenger or booking placed on the waitlist for a specific fare class bucket when availability is closed. Captures the PNR reference, requested cabin and fare class, waitlist priority rank, waitlist reason (sold-out, upgrade request, group overflow), creation timestamp, and clearance status. Managed by Altéa Inventory\'s waitlist processing engine and cleared automatically when bucket availability opens.'
}

Table finance.general_ledger {
  general_ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each general ledger journal entry line item record in the SAP S/4HANA FI-GL module. Serves as the immutable primary key for the silver layer lakehouse table.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each GL entry belongs to ONE company code. The general_ledger table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. This is a mandatory dimension in SAP FI-GL.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each GL entry can be assigned to ONE cost center for CO allocation. The general_ledger table has cost_centre (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the cost_centre master.']
  accounting_principle varchar [note: 'The accounting standard under which this posting is recorded. Supports dual-reporting requirements for airlines operating across multiple jurisdictions. IFRS16 specifically tracks right-of-use asset and lease liability postings for aircraft operating leases.']
  amount_company_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The posting amount converted to the company code\'s local currency (BSEG-DMBTR). Used for statutory financial reporting, local GAAP compliance, and period-end balance sheet preparation.']
  amount_group_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The posting amount translated to the group reporting currency (BSEG-KURSF-based calculation, stored as parallel ledger amount). Supports consolidated financial statements and group-level CASK/RASK reporting across all airline entities.']
  amount_transaction_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross posting amount in the original transaction currency (BSEG-WRBTR). Represents the raw financial value before currency conversion. Used for interline billing settlements, BSP reconciliation, and multi-currency financial reporting.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Free-form assignment field used for sorting and grouping line items in account statements (BSEG-ZUONR). Typically populated with flight number, route code, or cost object reference to facilitate operational cost reconciliation.']
  company_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the company code\'s local (functional) currency (T001-WAERS). The currency in which statutory accounts are prepared for the legal entity.']
  cost_element varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost element corresponding to the GL account for cost and revenue postings (BSEG-KOSTL-linked KSTAR). Bridges FI and CO modules, enabling detailed cost analysis for fuel uplift, crew costs, MRO expenses, and lease payments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the accounting document was first created in SAP (BKPF-CPUDT + BKPF-CPUTM combined). Provides the immutable audit creation timestamp for the journal entry, distinct from the business posting_date.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'SAP indicator specifying whether the line item is a debit (S = Soll) or credit (H = Haben) posting (BSEG-SHKZG). Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping and ensures the document balances to zero.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date of the original source document (invoice, receipt, contract) that triggered the accounting entry (BKPF-BLDAT). May differ from posting_date for accruals, prepayments, or late-received invoices.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI accounting document number uniquely identifying the journal entry header within a company code and fiscal year. Corresponds to BKPF-BELNR in SAP. All line items sharing the same document number belong to the same posting event.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'SAP document type code classifying the nature of the accounting entry (BKPF-BLART). Controls number ranges and account types. Common airline values include SA (GL account document), KR (vendor invoice), DR (customer invoice), ZP (payment), AA (asset posting), AB (accounting document). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|KR|DR|ZP|AA|AB|RV|WA — promote to reference product]']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency amount to the company code currency at the time of posting (BKPF-KURSF). Critical for IFRS IAS 21 compliance and interline settlement reconciliation.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Accounting period (1–16) within the fiscal year to which the posting belongs (BKPF-MONAT in SAP). Periods 13–16 are special closing periods used for year-end adjustments, accruals, and audit corrections.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which the accounting document belongs (BKPF-GJAHR in SAP). Used for period-end close, annual financial reporting, and year-over-year variance analysis.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP functional area classifying the business function of the posting for cost-of-sales accounting (BSEG-FKBER). Enables income statement presentation by function (e.g., Flight Operations, Sales & Distribution, Administration) per IFRS IAS 1.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The GL account number from the chart of accounts to which this line item is posted (BSEG-HKONT / SKA1-SAKNR). Represents the financial classification of the transaction (e.g., fuel expense, lease liability, revenue, accrued liabilities). Core to CASK reporting and financial consolidation.']
  gl_account_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the GL account (SKA1-TXT50). Denormalised from the chart of accounts for reporting convenience and audit trail completeness. Examples: \'Jet Fuel Expense\', \'Aircraft Lease Liability (IFRS 16)\', \'Interline Receivable\'.']
  header_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description at the accounting document header level (BKPF-BKTXT). Provides overall context for the journal entry, shared across all line items of the same document. Used for audit trail and period-end close documentation.']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this posting is an intercompany transaction between two legal entities within the airline group (BKPF-BVTYP populated). True = intercompany posting requiring elimination in group consolidation. Critical for IFRS 10 consolidation.']
  internal_order varchar [note: 'SAP CO internal order number used to collect costs for specific projects, campaigns, or temporary cost objects (BSEG-AUFNR). Used in aviation for aircraft modification projects, route launch campaigns, and MRO work orders.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the accounting document was last modified in SAP. Tracks changes to parked documents, header text updates, or clearing events. Supports change audit trail requirements under SOX and IFRS.']
  ledger_code varchar [note: 'SAP parallel ledger identifier (BKPF-RLDNR) supporting dual IFRS and local GAAP reporting. Common values: \'0L\' = leading ledger (IFRS), \'N1\' = local GAAP ledger. Enables simultaneous reporting under multiple accounting standards required for multinational airline operations.']
  line_item_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the accounting document (BSEG-BUZEI in SAP). Identifies the specific debit or credit posting line within the journal entry. Combined with document_number and company_code forms the natural key of a GL line item.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the accounting document is posted to the general ledger (BKPF-BUDAT). Determines the fiscal period assignment and is the primary date used for financial reporting and period-end close.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accounting document. \'posted\' = active GL entry; \'reversed\' = entry has been reversed by a reversal document; \'parked\' = document saved but not yet posted; \'held\' = incomplete document; \'cleared\' = open item has been cleared against a payment or offsetting entry.']
  posting_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the individual line item posting (BSEG-SGTXT). Provides narrative context for the accounting entry, e.g., \'Jet fuel uplift JFK-LHR 15-Jan\', \'IFRS 16 lease liability accrual Q1\', \'Interline settlement IATA BSP Jan\'.']
  posting_user varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person or system process that created the accounting document (BKPF-USNAM). Essential for SOX audit trail, segregation of duties controls, and financial fraud detection. May be a batch job ID for automated postings.']
  profit_centre varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling profit centre representing a business unit responsible for its own profitability (BSEG-PRCTR / CEPC-PRCTR). Used for route-level, hub-level, or business-segment P&L reporting. Supports RASK and yield analysis by profit centre.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the source document (BKPF-XBLNR), such as a vendor invoice number, AWB (Air Waybill) number, PNR, interline billing statement number, or BSP settlement reference. Enables traceability from the GL back to the originating business transaction.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the corresponding reversal or reversed document (BKPF-STBLG). Populated when reversal_indicator is true, enabling bidirectional traceability between original and reversal entries for audit and compliance purposes.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this document is a reversal of a previously posted document (BKPF-STBLG is populated). True = this is a reversal document. Supports audit trail integrity and period-end close accuracy.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax amount (VAT/GST/PFC) associated with this line item in the transaction currency (BSEG-WMWST). Supports tax reconciliation, VAT return preparation, and Passenger Facility Charge (PFC) reporting to airport authorities.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'SAP tax code determining the applicable tax rate and tax account for the posting (BSEG-MWSKZ). Relevant for VAT/GST on airline ancillary services, ground handling charges, and MRO procurement. Supports tax reporting to national revenue authorities.']
  trading_partner_company_code varchar [note: 'Company code of the intercompany trading partner for intercompany postings (BSEG-VBUND). Populated when intercompany_indicator is true. Used for automated intercompany elimination during group financial consolidation.']
  transaction_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the original transaction (BSEG-WAERS). Identifies the currency in which the business event was denominated, e.g., USD for US routes, EUR for European operations, GBP for UK operations.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP Project System WBS element for project-based cost assignment (BSEG-PS_PSP_PNR). Used for capital expenditure projects such as fleet acquisition, terminal construction, and major MRO overhauls. Supports IFRS IAS 16 capitalisation tracking.']

  Note: 'SAP S/4HANA FI-GL SSOT for all accounting entries across the airlines chart of accounts. Each record represents a journal entry document (posting) with header attributes (document number, posting date, fiscal period, document type, company code, reference, reversal indicator, posting user) and line items (debit/credit amounts, GL account, cost centre, profit centre, functional area, cost element, currency, exchange rate). Supports IFRS and local GAAP dual reporting, period-end close, intercompany postings, accruals, provisions, and financial consolidation across all legal entities. Serves as the immutable audit trail for regulatory financial reporting and the single authoritative source for all financial postings.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each journal entry record in the general ledger. Primary key for the journal_entry data product in the finance domain silver layer.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each journal entry belongs to ONE company code (legal entity). The journal_entry table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. Company code is a mandat']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each journal entry can be assigned to ONE cost center for CO allocation. The journal_entry table has cost_center (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the cost_centre master. This is a standa']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Each journal entry posts to ONE GL account. The journal_entry table has gl_account_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the general_ledger master. This is a standard SAP FI relationship']
  account_type varchar [note: 'SAP FI account type indicating the subledger category for this posting: A=Asset, D=Customer (Debtor/Accounts Receivable), K=Vendor (Creditor/Accounts Payable), M=Material, S=General Ledger. Determines which subledger is updated.']
  amount_document_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The transaction amount in the original document currency (transaction currency). May differ from amount_local_currency when the source document is in a foreign currency, such as interline billing settlements in USD or EUR.']
  amount_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross transaction amount in the company code\'s local (functional) currency. This is the primary monetary value for statutory reporting and forms part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this transaction.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI assignment field used for sorting and matching open items during clearing (e.g., flight number, route code, cost object reference). Supports automated payment matching and interline settlement reconciliation.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'SAP FI business area code representing an organizational unit for which an internal balance sheet and P&L can be produced (e.g., passenger operations, cargo, MRO, loyalty). Supports segment reporting under IFRS 8.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'The date on which this open item was cleared (matched against a payment or offsetting entry). Used for accounts receivable aging analysis, days sales outstanding (DSO) calculation, and cash flow reporting.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI document number of the clearing transaction that matched and closed this open item (e.g., payment document clearing an invoice, credit memo clearing a debit). Populated when posting_status is \'cleared\'.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this journal entry line is a debit (D) or credit (C) posting. Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping and ensures the general ledger remains in balance. Aligns with SAP FI posting key logic.']
  document_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the original document currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Critical for interline billing, BSP settlements, and multi-currency treasury operations.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date of the original source document (invoice date, payment date, contract date) that triggered this journal entry. May differ from posting_date when documents are processed with a lag.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally visible SAP FI document number uniquely identifying this accounting document within the company code and fiscal year. Used as the primary business reference for audit trail and reconciliation.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'SAP FI document type code classifying the nature of the accounting document (e.g., SA=General Ledger, KR=Vendor Invoice, DR=Customer Invoice, ZP=Payment, AB=Accounting Document). Drives number range assignment and posting rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|KR|DR|ZP|AB|RE|RV|KZ|DZ — promote to reference product]']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this journal entry record was created in the SAP S/4HANA system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED timestamp for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign currency exchange rate applied to translate the document currency amount to the local currency amount at the time of posting. Sourced from SAP exchange rate tables and aligned with treasury hedging policies.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The accounting period (1–16, including special periods 13–16 for year-end adjustments) within the fiscal year in which this journal entry is posted. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA posting period control.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The accounting fiscal year in which this journal entry is recorded, as defined by the airline\'s fiscal year variant in SAP S/4HANA. May differ from the calendar year for airlines with non-calendar fiscal years.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP CO functional area classifying the cost by business function (e.g., PROD=Production/Flight Operations, SELL=Sales, ADMN=Administration, MAINT=Maintenance). Supports cost-of-sales accounting and CASK component analysis.']
  header_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description at the document header level providing business context for the journal entry (e.g., \'Monthly fuel accrual JFK hub\', \'IFRS 16 lease liability Q3\', \'Interline BSP settlement October\'). Supports audit review and financial analysis.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP CO internal order number used to collect costs for specific projects or events (e.g., aircraft heavy maintenance C-check, airport terminal renovation, fleet acquisition project). Enables project-level cost tracking.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this journal entry record was last modified in the source system. Supports incremental data loading, change data capture, and audit trail requirements.']
  line_item_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description at the individual line item level providing specific context for this debit or credit posting (e.g., \'Jet-A fuel uplift LHR 15-Oct\', \'ACMI wet lease payment\', \'Crew hotel deadhead expense\').']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the company code\'s local (functional) currency. Defines the base currency for statutory financial reporting for the legal entity.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger and determines the fiscal period assignment. This is the authoritative accounting date for period-end close and financial reporting.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'SAP FI two-digit posting key controlling the account type, debit/credit indicator, and field selection for this line item (e.g., 40=GL Debit, 50=GL Credit, 31=Vendor Credit, 01=Customer Debit). Determines the accounting treatment applied.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the journal entry in the general ledger workflow. \'Posted\' indicates a completed, immutable entry; \'parked\' is a draft pending approval; \'reversed\' indicates a correcting entry has been applied; \'cleared\' indicates the open item has been matched.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO profit center identifying the business segment (e.g., route, hub, cabin class, cargo) responsible for this posting. Supports profitability analysis by route, fleet type, and business unit.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the originating source document (e.g., vendor invoice number, PNR, AWB number, interline billing statement number, lease contract reference). Enables cross-system reconciliation and audit trail linkage.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal document (True) or an original posting (False). Reversal entries are created to correct erroneous postings and maintain audit integrity in the general ledger.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax component (VAT, GST, PFC, or other applicable taxes) included in or associated with this journal entry posting. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment component. Supports tax reporting and PFC (Passenger Facility Charge) reconciliation.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'SAP tax code identifying the applicable tax type and rate for this posting (e.g., V1=Input VAT 19%, A1=Output VAT 19%). Drives automatic tax calculation and tax return preparation.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP PS Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element for project-based cost assignments, such as aircraft acquisition, fleet modification programs, or major infrastructure projects. Supports capital expenditure tracking under IFRS IAS 16.']

  Note: 'Individual accounting posting records in the general ledger representing debits and credits for every financial transaction across the airline. Captures document type, posting date, fiscal period, currency, exchange rate, reference document, reversal indicators, and posting user. Aligns with SAP FI document posting model and supports audit trail for regulatory financial reporting.'
}

Table finance.cost_centre {
  cost_centre_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the cost centre record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the cost_centre data product.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each cost center belongs to ONE company code. The cost_centre table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. Cost centers are assigned to company codes ']
  activity_type_code varchar [note: 'Primary SAP CO activity type associated with this cost centre, defining the unit of output for internal activity allocation (e.g., \'FLT-HRS\' = flight hours, \'BLK-HRS\' = block hours, \'MRO-MHR\' = maintenance man-hours, \'GND-TRN\' = ground turns). Used to calculate internal activity rates for cost allocation between centres.']
  annual_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved annual budget allocated to this cost centre for the current fiscal year, in the controlling area currency. Represents the authorised spending limit for departmental P&L management. Used in budget vs. actual variance reporting and financial planning.']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'SAP CO planning version identifier for the budget associated with this cost centre (e.g., \'000\' = actuals, \'001\' = original budget, \'002\' = revised forecast, \'PLAN\' = annual plan). Enables comparison of actuals vs. budget vs. forecast in variance reporting.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI business area assigned to this cost centre, representing a distinct area of operations for internal balance sheet and P&L reporting (e.g., \'PASS\' = Passenger, \'CARG\' = Cargo, \'MRO\' = Maintenance). Enables segment reporting per IFRS 8 without full profit centre accounting.']
  cask_allocation_key varchar [note: 'Allocation driver used to apportion this cost centre\'s costs to Available Seat Kilometers (ASK) for CASK reporting. \'ask\' = direct ASK-based allocation; \'block_hours\' = by block hours flown; \'departures\' = by number of departures; \'pax\' = by passenger count; \'revenue\' = by revenue share; \'equal\' = equal split; \'manual\' = manually defined percentages. Critical for unit cost benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ask|block_hours|departures|pax|revenue|equal|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  category_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost centre category that classifies the functional nature of the centre (e.g., \'F\' = Flight Operations, \'M\' = Maintenance, \'G\' = Ground Handling, \'A\' = Administration, \'C\' = Cargo, \'L\' = Loyalty). Controls which activity types and statistical key figures can be posted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: F|M|G|A|C|L|S|T — promote to reference product]']
  centre_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the responsibility centre by financial accountability type. \'cost\' centres track expenditure only (e.g., maintenance base); \'profit\' centres track both revenue and cost for P&L reporting (e.g., route group, cargo division); \'investment\' centres include capital employed; \'revenue\' centres track revenue attribution (e.g., lounge, ancillary). Drives CASK/RASK allocation methodology.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO controlling area to which this cost centre belongs. The controlling area defines the organisational unit within which cost accounting is performed (e.g., \'AA01\' for the airline group). Determines currency, fiscal year variant, and chart of accounts applicable to this centre.']
  corsia_emissions_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost centre is subject to CORSIA (Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation) emissions monitoring and offset cost accounting. True for international route operations cost centres. Drives inclusion in ICAO CORSIA compliance reporting and carbon offset cost allocation.']
  cost_centre_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the cost centre within the SAP CO controlling area. Used in journal entries, budget allocations, and management reporting (e.g., \'FLT-OPS-001\', \'MRO-BASE-LHR\', \'CARGO-HUB-JFK\'). Aligns with SAP CO cost centre number.']
  cost_centre_description varchar [note: 'Long-form narrative description of the cost centre\'s business purpose, scope of operations, and cost accountability. Provides context for financial analysts and auditors beyond the short name (e.g., \'Responsible for all direct operating costs of widebody long-haul fleet on North Atlantic routes including fuel uplift, crew costs, and ATC charges\').']
  cost_centre_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the cost centre as maintained in SAP CO master data (e.g., \'Flight Operations — North Atlantic Hub\', \'Cabin Crew Training — Base London\', \'MRO Line Maintenance — Frankfurt\'). Used in financial reports and management dashboards.']
  cost_centre_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost centre in SAP CO. \'active\' = open for postings; \'inactive\' = temporarily suspended; \'locked\' = blocked for new postings (period-end); \'pending_activation\' = created but not yet released; \'closed\' = permanently deactivated with no further postings permitted.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cost centre master record was first created in the source SAP CO system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with financial record retention requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost centre\'s controlling currency (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Determines the currency in which budgets are planned, actuals are recorded, and variance reports are produced. May differ from company code currency for international operations.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which the cost centre\'s budget and planning data applies (e.g., 2024). Aligns with the SAP CO fiscal year variant configured for the controlling area. Used to version budget records and support multi-year trend analysis.']
  fuel_cost_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost centre is designated for fuel cost accounting and uplift tracking. True for flight operations and route cost centres where jet fuel (Jet-A/A1) costs are directly attributed. Enables fuel cost per ASK (fuel CASK) component reporting and hedging P&L attribution.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO functional area classifying the cost centre by business function for cost-of-sales accounting and segment reporting (e.g., \'PROD\' = Production/Operations, \'SELL\' = Sales, \'ADMN\' = Administration, \'R&D\' = Research). Required for IFRS income statement by function of expense.']
  gl_account_group varchar [note: 'SAP FI general ledger account group associated with primary cost postings for this cost centre. Defines the range of GL accounts permissible for postings, ensuring cost classification consistency (e.g., \'OPEX\' for operating expenses, \'FUEL\' for fuel costs, \'LABOR\' for crew and staff costs).']
  hierarchy_area varchar [note: 'Node identifier within the standard cost centre hierarchy (SAP CO standard hierarchy) to which this centre is assigned. Enables roll-up reporting by business segment, division, or region (e.g., \'FLIGHT-OPS\', \'MRO-EMEA\', \'CARGO-APAC\'). Used for group-level CASK reporting and segment P&L consolidation.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this cost centre within the standard hierarchy tree (1 = top-level division, 2 = sub-division, 3 = department, 4 = operational unit, etc.). Used to control report aggregation and drill-down navigation in management accounting dashboards.']
  hub_airport_code varchar [note: 'IATA three-letter airport code of the hub or base airport associated with this cost centre, where applicable (e.g., \'LHR\' for London Heathrow hub operations, \'JFK\' for New York hub, \'FRA\' for Frankfurt MRO base). Enables geographic cost attribution and hub-level CASK analysis. Null for non-location-specific centres.']
  iata_cost_category varchar [note: 'IATA standard cost category classification for airline management accounting and industry benchmarking (e.g., \'Flight Operations\', \'Maintenance\', \'Passenger Services\', \'Sales and Distribution\', \'General and Administration\'). Enables CASK component benchmarking against IATA industry data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Flight Operations|Maintenance|Passenger Services|Sales and Distribution|General and Administration|Cargo|Ground Operations — promote to reference product]']
  ifrs16_lease_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost centre carries IFRS 16 right-of-use asset and lease liability obligations (e.g., aircraft operating leases, airport gate leases, hangar leases). True triggers inclusion in IFRS 16 lease liability reporting and depreciation of right-of-use assets. Critical for airline balance sheet compliance.']
  interline_settlement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost centre participates in interline billing settlement processes (IATA BSP/ARC). True for cost centres associated with interline revenue sharing, prorate agreements, and bilateral settlement with partner carriers. Drives inclusion in interline financial reconciliation workflows.']
  internal_activity_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Planned cost rate per unit of activity output for this cost centre (in controlling area currency). Used for internal cost allocation between cost centres (e.g., cost per block hour charged from Flight Operations to Route cost centres). Recalculated each planning cycle. Classified confidential as it reflects internal transfer pricing.']
  last_changed_by varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who last modified this cost centre master record. Supports audit trail requirements for financial master data governance and SOX compliance controls over cost centre maintenance.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this cost centre master record in SAP CO. Used for change tracking, incremental data extraction (delta loads), and audit compliance. Corresponds to SAP CDHDR change document header timestamp.']
  profit_centre_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO profit centre to which this cost centre is assigned for internal P&L reporting. Enables revenue attribution and profitability analysis at route, hub, or business segment level. Supports RASK/CASK analysis by profit centre.']
  rask_attribution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost centre participates in Revenue per Available Seat Kilometer (RASK) attribution for route profitability analysis. True for profit centres and revenue-generating units (e.g., route groups, lounges, ancillary services); False for pure cost centres (e.g., corporate functions, back-office).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code to which this cost centre is assigned for regional management reporting (e.g., \'EMEA\', \'NORAM\', \'APAC\', \'LATAM\', \'DOM\' for domestic). Supports regional P&L roll-up, CASK benchmarking by region, and DOT/EASA regional regulatory reporting.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated name of the cost centre used in condensed reports, dashboards, and system displays where space is limited. Corresponds to SAP CO short text field (up to 20 characters).']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which this cost centre is valid and open for financial postings in SAP CO. Supports time-dependent master data management — cost centre attributes may change over time with new validity periods. Aligns with fiscal year planning cycles.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date until which this cost centre is valid for financial postings. Null or 31-Dec-9999 indicates an open-ended validity. Used to manage cost centre lifecycle, seasonal operations (e.g., charter season cost centres), and organisational restructuring.']

  Note: 'SAP CO organisational unit master representing cost-incurring and profit-bearing responsibility centres within the airline — e.g., flight operations, cabin crew, MRO line/base stations, ground handling, airport lounges, cargo division, route groups (by hub, region), and corporate functions. Stores centre type (cost/profit), hierarchy level and parent, responsible manager, controlling area, validity periods, budget allocation references, revenue attribution rules, CASK/RASK allocation keys, and internal activity rates. Enables departmental P&L reporting, CASK component attribution, route profitability analysis, and management accounting by business segment.'
}

Table finance.accounts_payable {
  accounts_payable_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for each vendor invoice record in the accounts payable ledger. Generated by the Databricks Silver Layer ingestion pipeline from SAP FI-AP document entries.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each AP invoice belongs to ONE company code. The accounts_payable table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. This is a mandatory dimension in SAP FI']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center to which this invoice expense is allocated, supporting CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) reporting and departmental cost accounting within SAP CO.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: AP invoices can be for the purchase or capitalization of fixed assets (aircraft, engines, ground equipment). A FK from accounts_payable to fixed_asset links the payment obligation to the asset being a']
  fleet_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.fleet_order. Business justification: Aircraft purchase progress payments and delivery milestone invoices are AP transactions that must reference the originating fleet order for three-way matching, CAPEX payment tracking, and purchase ord']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: AP invoices and payments generate GL postings in SAP FI-AP. Every accounts_payable record corresponds to one or more GL entries. This FK normalizes the relationship between the AP sub-ledger and the g']
  ground_handler_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to airport.ground_handler. Business justification: AP invoices are issued by ground handlers for ramp, baggage, and passenger handling services. Linking AP to the ground_handler vendor record enables vendor payment tracking, SLA-linked invoice validat']
  lease_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.lease_contract. Business justification: AP invoices for IFRS 16 lease payments should be linked to the lease contract. The accounts_payable table has ifrs16_lease_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating lease-related invoices, but no explicit FK. Adding ']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the invoice within the internal approval workflow, reflecting authorization levels required before payment execution. Supports segregation of duties and internal controls per SOX compliance requirements.']
  bsp_arc_settlement_ref varchar [note: 'The IATA Billing and Settlement Plan (BSP) or Airlines Reporting Corporation (ARC) settlement reference number for invoices settled through interline or agency billing cycles. Applicable for GDS provider fees, interline billing, and travel agency commission settlements.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP accounting document number of the payment document that cleared (settled) this open invoice item. Populated upon successful payment execution. Corresponds to SAP field AUGBL (Clearing Document). Null for unpaid invoices.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this accounts payable record was first created in the SAP FI-AP system, representing the initial document entry time. Used for audit trail, SLA monitoring of invoice processing cycle time, and data lineage.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cash discount amount available if payment is made by the discount_due_date, as negotiated in the vendor payment terms. Supports working capital optimization and supplier relationship management.']
  discount_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which early payment must be made to qualify for the cash discount offered under the vendor\'s payment terms (e.g., 2/10 net 30). Supports cash flow optimization and working capital management.']
  due_date date [note: 'The calculated date by which payment must be made to the vendor to comply with agreed payment terms and avoid late payment penalties. Derived from invoice date plus payment term days. Corresponds to SAP field FAEDT (Net Due Date).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The exchange rate applied to translate the invoice currency amount to the local currency at the time of posting. Sourced from SAP exchange rate table (TCURR). Critical for FX exposure reporting and treasury operations.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal posting period (1–16, including special periods) within the fiscal year in which this invoice is recorded. Supports monthly accrual management and period-end close processes in SAP FI.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this vendor invoice is posted in SAP FI. Used for period-end closing, financial reporting, and year-over-year cost analysis. Corresponds to SAP field GJAHR.']
  fuel_uplift_volume_liters decimal(12,2) [note: 'For fuel vendor invoices, the volume of jet fuel (Jet-A or Jet-A1) uplifted in liters as recorded on the fuel receipt. Supports fuel cost per liter analysis, CASK fuel component reporting, and CORSIA carbon emissions accounting. Null for non-fuel invoices.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The SAP general ledger account number to which the invoice expense is posted, enabling cost classification for financial reporting and CASK component analysis (e.g., fuel cost GL, MRO cost GL, airport charges GL). Corresponds to SAP field HKONT.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which goods or services were confirmed as received, forming the third leg of the three-way match (PO → Goods Receipt → Invoice). Critical for MRO parts, fuel uplift, and catering deliveries. Corresponds to SAP MIGO goods receipt posting date.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross amount of the vendor invoice before deduction of taxes, withholding tax, or discounts, expressed in the invoice currency. Represents the full obligation as stated on the vendor\'s invoice document.']
  ifrs16_lease_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice relates to a lease obligation subject to IFRS 16 Leases accounting treatment (e.g., aircraft dry lease, wet lease, ACMI lease payments, airport gate lease). When true, the payment is classified as a lease liability repayment rather than an operating expense.']
  invoice_currency varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the vendor invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). May differ from the company code local currency, requiring foreign exchange translation for GL posting. Corresponds to SAP field WAERS.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date printed on the vendor\'s invoice document, representing the business event date for the supply of goods or services. Used as the baseline for payment term calculation and tax point determination. Corresponds to SAP field BLDAT.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The externally-assigned invoice number as printed on the vendor\'s invoice document. Used for vendor reconciliation, dispute resolution, and audit trail. Corresponds to SAP field BELNR (Accounting Document Number) or XBLNR (Reference Document Number).']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor invoice within the SAP FI-AP workflow. \'open\' = posted and awaiting payment; \'parked\' = saved but not yet posted; \'blocked\' = payment block applied; \'in_approval\' = pending workflow approval; \'cleared\' = fully paid and cleared; \'cancelled\' = reversed/cancelled document.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this accounts payable record, capturing changes to approval status, payment block, or clearing information. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net payable amount translated into the company code\'s local (functional) currency using the exchange rate at posting date. Used for GL posting, financial reporting, and CASK calculation in the airline\'s reporting currency.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount payable to the vendor after deducting withholding tax and any applicable cash discounts from the gross invoice amount. Represents the actual cash outflow obligation. Calculated as gross_amount minus withholding_tax_amount minus discount_amount.']
  payment_block_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why a payment block has been applied to this invoice, preventing it from being included in the next payment run. Corresponds to SAP field ZLSPR (Payment Block Key). Null when no block is active.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date on which payment was executed to the vendor, either via bank transfer, cheque, or BSP/ARC settlement. Populated upon payment run completion. Corresponds to SAP clearing document value date.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which payment is executed to the vendor. \'bank_transfer\' = SWIFT/SEPA wire; \'cheque\' = paper cheque; \'bsp_settlement\' = IATA Billing and Settlement Plan net settlement; \'arc_settlement\' = Airlines Reporting Corporation settlement; \'direct_debit\' = automated debit. Corresponds to SAP field ZLSCH.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'The SAP payment terms key (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10N30) defining the agreed payment schedule, discount percentages, and due date calculation rules for this vendor. Corresponds to SAP field ZTERM.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor invoice is posted to the SAP general ledger, determining the accounting period for recognition. May differ from invoice date due to processing delays. Corresponds to SAP field BUDAT.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'The internal SAP FI accounting document number (BELNR) assigned upon posting of the vendor invoice in the general ledger. Uniquely identifies the FI document within a company code and fiscal year.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount (VAT, GST, or applicable local tax) charged on the vendor invoice. Corresponds to SAP field WMWST. Used for input tax recovery and tax reporting to national revenue authorities.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The SAP tax code identifying the applicable tax rate and tax type (input VAT, zero-rated, exempt) for this invoice line. Drives automated tax calculation and input tax recovery reporting. Corresponds to SAP field MWSKZ.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Result of the automated three-way match validation comparing the purchase order, goods receipt, and vendor invoice for quantity and price alignment. \'matched\' = all three documents agree; \'price_variance\' = invoice price differs from PO; \'quantity_variance\' = invoice quantity differs from GR; \'pending_gr\' = goods receipt not yet confirmed; \'failed\' = match failed requiring manual review.']
  vendor_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor type supplying goods or services to the airline. Drives cost allocation to CASK components and supports spend analytics by supplier category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mro_supplier|fuel_vendor|airport_authority|gds_provider|catering|ground_handling|bsp_arc — promote to reference product]']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of withholding tax (WHT) to be deducted from the vendor payment and remitted to the tax authority on behalf of the vendor. Applicable for cross-border supplier payments subject to bilateral tax treaties. Corresponds to SAP Extended Withholding Tax (WITH_ITEM).']

  Note: 'SAP FI-AP vendor invoice and payment obligation records for the airline covering MRO suppliers, fuel vendors, airport authorities, GDS providers, catering contractors, and ground handling agents. Each record represents a vendor invoice document with invoice number, vendor ID, invoice date, line items, tax amounts, purchase order reference, goods receipt reference, three-way match status, payment terms, due dates, withholding tax, approval workflow status, payment execution details (method, bank, value date, clearing document), and BSP/ARC settlement obligations. Supports procure-to-pay lifecycle, cash flow forecasting, and supplier payment runs.'
}

Table finance.accounts_receivable {
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each accounts receivable record in the SAP FI-AR module. Primary key for the silver layer lakehouse table.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each AR document belongs to ONE company code. The accounts_receivable table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. This is a mandatory dimension in SA']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: AR invoices and collections generate GL postings in SAP FI-AR. Every accounts_receivable record corresponds to GL entries. This FK normalizes the AR sub-ledger to general ledger relationship, consiste']
  interline_billing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.interline_billing. Business justification: Interline billing settlements generate AR records when partner airlines owe the airline money. An AR record for interline revenue should reference the originating interline_billing record. This FK lin']
  ageing_bucket varchar [note: 'Ageing classification of the open receivable based on days past due date: CURRENT (not yet due), 1_30_DAYS, 31_60_DAYS, 61_90_DAYS, 91_120_DAYS, OVER_120_DAYS. Drives credit risk assessment and bad debt provisioning under IFRS 9 expected credit loss model.']
  ar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receivable: OPEN (unpaid), PARTIALLY_PAID (partial payment received), CLEARED (fully paid and matched), DISPUTED (customer dispute raised), WRITTEN_OFF (bad debt write-off applied), IN_DUNNING (active dunning process initiated).']
  bank_value_date date [note: 'Value date on which funds were credited to the airline\'s bank account for this receivable. Distinct from posting date; used for treasury cash flow forecasting and bank reconciliation.']
  bsp_arc_billing_period varchar [note: 'IATA BSP or ARC billing period identifier (e.g., 2024-W03 for weekly cycle) to which this agency receivable belongs. Supports settlement reconciliation and dispute management with travel agencies.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date on which the receivable was fully cleared (matched against incoming payment or credit memo) in SAP. Null for open items. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP document number of the payment or credit memo that cleared this receivable. Enables end-to-end payment reconciliation and audit trail from invoice to settlement.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'SAP cost center associated with the receivable for internal cost allocation and CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) reporting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the AR record was first created in the source SAP FI-AR system. Provides audit trail for record origination and supports data lineage in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  credit_control_area varchar [note: 'SAP credit control area (KKBER) under which the customer\'s credit limit is managed. Groups customers for credit exposure monitoring across company codes.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved credit limit assigned to the customer in the credit control area, expressed in the credit control area currency. Used for credit exposure monitoring and order block decisions.']
  customer_account_number varchar [note: 'SAP debtor account number (KUNNR) identifying the customer — travel agency, corporate account, interline carrier, or cargo shipper — against whom the receivable is raised.']
  customer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the debtor customer as maintained in SAP customer master. Covers travel agencies, corporate accounts, interline carriers, and cargo clients.']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the receivable is past its due date as of the last refresh date. Negative values indicate days until due. Used for DSO reporting and credit risk monitoring.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the reason for a customer dispute on this receivable (e.g., pricing discrepancy, duplicate billing, service not rendered, ADM — Agency Debit Memo dispute). Null when no dispute exists. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PRICE_DISCREPANCY|DUPLICATE_BILLING|SERVICE_NOT_RENDERED|ADM_DISPUTE|CREDIT_NOT_APPLIED|INTERLINE_PRORATE_DIFF — promote to reference product]']
  document_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the AR document amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency airline operations and foreign exchange reporting.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AR accounting document number uniquely identifying the receivable posting within the company code. Corresponds to BELNR field in SAP BSID/BSAD tables.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'SAP document type code classifying the nature of the AR posting: DR (customer invoice), DG (credit memo), DA (customer document), RV (billing document transfer), AB (accounting document). Drives posting logic and account determination.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is contractually due from the customer, calculated from invoice date plus payment terms (e.g., BSP weekly settlement cycle, net 30 corporate terms). Key input for ageing bucket classification and dunning.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current dunning level (1–4) assigned to this receivable in SAP dunning program, indicating the escalation stage of the collection notice. Level 0 means no dunning initiated.']
